BNSS_SCH1_R422:058bcce1c9ab20a2ac4672f21: Section 344: Falsification of accounts. Imprisonment for — 7 years, or fine, or both.

BNSS_SCH1_R430:41f96515718ab9f056f7d9450: Section 350( 2: ) Making use of any such false mark. — Imprisonment for 3 years, or fine, or both.
BNSS_SCH1_R431:52bf5923a3207586f94654907: Section 351( 2: ) Criminal intimidation. — Imprisonment for 2 years, or fine, or both.
BNSS_SCH1_R432:d888e5af4de747a7d9011c52c: Section 351( 3: ) If threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, etc. — Imprisonment for 7 years, or fine, or both.

BNSS_SCH1_R435:87cf3ac08474f899307d61685: Section 353( 1: ) False statement, rumour, etc., circulated with intent to cause mutiny or offen... — Imprisonment for 3 years, or fine, or both.
BNSS_SCH1_R436:189b8801949ddddd026207521: Section 353( 2: ) False statement, rumour, etc., with intent to create enmity, hatred or ill-wil... — Imprisonment for 3 years, or fine, or both.
BNSS_SCH1_R437:ded8f3ccfd0aa519a67a02db4: Section 353( 3: ) False statement, rumour, etc., made in place of worship, etc., with intent to ... — Imprisonment for 5 years and fine.

BNSS_SCH1_R439:7c887550cd1e3defb8654e07d: Section 355: Appearing in a public place, etc., in a state of intoxication, and causing annoy... — Simple imprisonment for 24 hours, or fine of 1,000 rupees, o...

BNSS_SCH1_R443:567916d820d42f1f3de272d60: Section 357: Being bound to attend on or supply the wants of a person who is helpless from yo... — Imprisonment for 3 months, or fine of 5,000 rupees, or both.
BNSS_SCH1_R444:f73c1d0ee98fcd77d6778c8b6: Section 7 years.: If punishable with death, imprisonment for life, or imprisonment for more than 7... — Cognizable. Cognizable.
BNS_S4:4fc94caf3f426792f3b779b79315b87b0: Section 4 enumerates the punishments to which offenders are liable under the provisions of this Sanhita. These comprise Death, Imprisonment for life, and Imprisonment, which is categorized into two descriptions: Rigorous (with hard labour) and Simple. Additionally, the section provides for Forfeiture of property, Fine, and Community Service.
BNS_S5:5a9c0d19a8a4a07f803d809f30dff95e1: The appropriate Government may, without the consent of the offender, commute any punishment under this Sanhita to any other punishment in accordance with section 474 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023. An Explanation defines "appropriate Government" as the Central Government for a sentence of death or offences against laws relating to matters to which the executive power of the Union extends. For offences against laws relating to matters to which the executive power of the State extends, the appropriate Government is the Government of the State within which the offender is sentenced, whether the sentence is of death or not.
BNS_S6:daad5b7bfc61b6d949cb0e7a86b263d1c: Section 6 stipulates the mechanism for calculating fractions of terms of punishment. It mandates that imprisonment for life shall be reckoned as equivalent to imprisonment for twenty years unless otherwise provided.
BNS_S10:6972f5a0602a02ce2549ceebd4a6b64d: Section 10 mandates that in all cases where a judgment determines a person is guilty of one of several specified offences, but it is doubtful of which of these offences he is guilty, the offender shall be punished for the offence for which the lowest punishment is provided. This mechanism applies specifically if the same punishment is not provided for all specified offences.
BNS_S9:40c8dcf68217e6e83f417215af277f878: Section 9 stipulates that where an offence is made up of parts, any of which parts is itself an offence, the offender shall not be punished with the punishment of more than one of such his offences, unless it be so expressly provided. Furthermore, where an offence falls within two or more separate definitions of any law in force, or where several acts combined constitute a different offence, the offender shall not be punished with a more severe punishment than the Court which tries him could award for any one of such offences.
BNS_S12:2a1922bd82733008b571cdbd902a8425: Section 12 stipulates that in executing a sentence of solitary confinement, such confinement shall in no case exceed fourteen days at a time, with intervals between the periods of solitary confinement of not less duration than such periods. When the imprisonment awarded exceeds three months, the solitary confinement shall not exceed seven days in any one month of the whole imprisonment awarded. Such periods must be separated by intervals of not less duration than such periods.
BNS_S14:53e114e6ef7d40b0c27abda2b0bfda2a: Section 14 provides that nothing is an offence when performed by a person who is bound by law, or who, acting in good faith, believes themselves bound by law due to a mistake of fact and not by reason of a mistake of law. The section includes illustrations involving a soldier firing on a mob under a superior officer's order in conformity with the law and a Court officer arresting an incorrect individual after due enquiry.
BNS_S16:582a88a68084f7c1c181499627a710e8: Section 16 stipulates that nothing done in pursuance of, or warranted by the judgment or order of, a Court is an offence, provided it is performed whilst such judgment or order remains in force. This protection applies notwithstanding that the Court may have had no jurisdiction to pass such judgment or order. This is subject to the proviso that the person doing the act in good faith believes that the Court had such jurisdiction.
BNS_S11:b090fa9751293df20cab168624765025: When any person is convicted of an offence for which the Court has power to sentence him to rigorous imprisonment, the Court may, by its sentence, order solitary confinement for any portion or portions of the imprisonment, not exceeding three months in the whole. This is applied according to a scale: (a) a time not exceeding one month if the term of imprisonment shall not exceed six months; (b) a time not exceeding two months if the term of imprisonment shall exceed six months and shall not exceed one year; and (c) a time not exceeding three months if the term of imprisonment shall exceed one year.
BNS_S18:61a07619c0afb8447c5ee0889709b4d2: Section 18 stipulates that nothing is an offence which is done by accident or misfortune, provided it is done without any criminal intention or knowledge in the doing of a lawful act in a lawful manner by lawful means and with proper care and caution. The section includes an illustration where the killing of a man by a flying hatchet head is excusable if there was no want of proper caution.
BNS_S20:5072b2955144a6cc0df9688f9a8d5988: Section 20 stipulates that nothing is an offence which is done by a child under seven years of age. This section contains no Provisos, Exceptions, or Explanations.
BNS_S15:0d42127a6e8b4f9ab59d2f79309b7514: Section 15 stipulates that nothing is an offence which is done by a Judge when acting judicially. This applies to the exercise of any power which is, or which in good faith he believes to be, given to him by law.
BNS_S22:1b8e6964417f587cb78aa804441d42b1: Section 22 provides that nothing is an offence which is done by a person who, at the time of doing it, is by reason of unsoundness of mind incapable of knowing the nature of the act. This applies if the person is likewise incapable of knowing that he is doing what is either wrong or contrary to law.
BNS_S17:8365a3298cf6c63c7e681e8e56b99334: Section 17 stipulates that nothing is an offence when an act is done by a person justified by law, or who, by reason of a mistake of fact and not by reason of a mistake of law in good faith, believes himself to be justified by law in doing it. The section includes an illustration where a person exercising the power to apprehend murderers in good faith commits no offence, even if the apprehended individual was actually acting in self-defence.
BNS_S24:0907903dfad56bb3e746f109aaf90f0a: Section 24 stipulates that for an offence requiring a particular intent or knowledge, a person acting in a state of intoxication shall be liable to be dealt with as if they possessed the same knowledge as if they had not been intoxicated. This liability is applicable unless the substance which intoxicated the person was administered to him without his knowledge or against his will.
BNS_S26:cb9df774f2e4195fa415ab9c04dc1bf6: Section 26 stipulates that an act not intended to cause death is not an offence by reason of any harm it may cause, or be intended or known by the doer to be likely to cause, provided it is done in good faith for the person's benefit. This protection applies if the person has given express or implied consent to suffer that harm or take the risk of that harm. The section includes an illustration wherein a surgeon performing an operation likely to cause death, without intending to cause death and acting in good faith for the patient's benefit with their consent, commits no offence.
BNS_S21:0f276588c12790653255d51cfb465baa: Section 21 states that nothing is an offence which is done by a child above seven years of age and under twelve years of age. This applies to a child who has not attained sufficient maturity of understanding to judge of the nature and consequences of his conduct on that occasion.
BNS_S28:0100b9e60f12d764dd6c5c4053779af1: Consent is not such a consent as is intended by any section of this Sanhita if given under fear of injury or under a misconception of fact, provided the person doing the act knows, or has reason to believe, the consent was given in consequence of such fear or misconception. It is similarly invalid if given by a person unable to understand the nature and consequence of the consent due to unsoundness of mind or intoxication. Furthermore, unless the contrary appears from the context, consent is not valid if given by a person who is under twelve years of age.
BNS_S30:42a9c2db902cf009cb677e888dc29dd2: Section 30 stipulates that no act is an offence due to harm caused to a person for whose benefit it is done in good faith without consent, provided the person cannot signify consent or is incapable of giving consent and lacks a guardian or person in lawful charge from whom consent could be obtained in time. A Proviso limits this exception, excluding the intentional causing or attempting to cause death; acts known to be likely to cause death for purposes other than preventing death or grievous hurt, or curing any grievous disease or infirmity; voluntary causing or attempting to cause hurt for purposes other than preventing death or hurt; and the abetment of any offence. Additionally, an Explanation clarifies that "mere pecuniary benefit" does not constitute "benefit" within the meaning of sections 26, 27, and 30.
BNS_S23:1d0ff2403a962bf098e713ef7a5748c6: Section 23 stipulates that nothing is an offence if a person, at the time of doing it, is, by reason of intoxication, incapable of knowing the nature of the act, or that he is doing what is either wrong, or contrary to law. This is subject to a proviso that the thing which intoxicated him was administered to him without his knowledge or against his will.
BNS_S34:4dc9c1e436b60f75bfd6f2e630d51981: Section 34, "Things done in private defence," stipulates that nothing is an offence which is done in the exercise of the right of private defence. This section contains no Provisos, Exceptions, or Explanations.
BNS_S42:9e144dbbfc5b3f5e6bbd7208280a1919: Section 42 provides that if the right of private defence is exercised due to the committing or attempting to commit theft, mischief, or criminal trespass not specified in section 41, such right does not extend to the voluntary causing of death. It does, however, extend to the voluntary causing to the wrong-doer of any harm other than death. This right is subject to the restrictions specified in section 37.
BNS_S25:63eb1a971d9ede8ff59288127e59e41c: Nothing which is not intended to cause death, or grievous hurt, and is not known by the doer to be likely to cause death or grievous hurt, is an offence by reason of harm caused or intended to any person above eighteen years of age who has given consent, whether express or implied, to suffer that harm. This non-offence status also extends to any harm which may be known by the doer to be likely to cause to any such person who has consented to take the risk of that harm.
BNS_S46:3cdd5900ed0b4d8e5a565c6a61c6e084: Section 46 defines an "Abettor" as a person who abets the commission of an offence or an act that would be an offence if committed by a person capable by law with the same intention or knowledge as the abettor. Explanation 1 specifies that abetment of the illegal omission of an act may be an offence even if the abettor is not bound to do that act, while Explanation 2 states the act abetted need not be committed nor the requisite effect caused to constitute the offence. Explanation 3 clarifies that the person abetted need not be capable by law of committing an offence or possess the same guilty intention or knowledge as the abettor. Explanation 4 stipulates that the abetment of an abetment is also an offence, and Explanation 5 provides that for abetment by conspiracy, it is sufficient if the abettor engages in the conspiracy, without needing to concert the offence with the person who commits it.
BNS_S27:f1a214a881894215f7aefbb6a402f74d: Section 27 provides that nothing done in good faith for the benefit of a person under twelve years of age, or person of unsound mind, by, or by either express or implied consent of the guardian or other person having lawful charge of that person, is an offence by reason of any harm which it may cause, or be intended by the doer to cause or be known by the doer to be likely to cause to that person. However, a Proviso stipulates that this exception shall not extend to the intentional causing of death or attempting to cause death, or doing anything known to be likely to cause death for any purpose other than the preventing of death or grievous hurt, or the curing of any grievous disease or infirmity. Furthermore, the Proviso excludes the voluntary causing of grievous hurt or attempting to cause grievous hurt, unless for the purpose of preventing death or grievous hurt, or the curing of any grievous disease or infirmity, and the abetment of any offence to which the exception would not extend.
BNS_S48:ae67fea4cc3d5d7e9d6fac0a7fd51505: Section 48 provides that a person abets an offence within the meaning of this Sanhita who, without and beyond India, abets the commission of any act in India which would constitute an offence if committed in India. The section includes an illustration whereby a person in country X who instigates another to commit murder in India is guilty of abetting murder.
BNS_S50:24421e260a1e39135619ec593c4079e7: Section 50 prescribes the punishment of abetment in cases where the person abetted commits the act with a different intention or knowledge from that of the abettor. The abettor shall be punished with the punishment provided for the offence which would have been committed if the act had been done with the intention or knowledge of the abettor and with no other.
BNS_S29:524392fe0e982708cad1b8da9e1ad3cb: Section 29 stipulates that the exceptions in sections 25, 26 and 27 do not extend to acts which are offences independently of any harm which they may cause, or be intended to cause, or be known to be likely to cause, to the person giving the consent, or on whose behalf the consent is given. An illustration clarifies that causing miscarriage, unless caused in good faith for the purpose of saving the life of the woman, is an offence independently of any harm; therefore, it is not an offence “by reason of such harm” and the consent of the woman or of her guardian does not justify the act.
BNS_S52:3e0284d5a4aad8c7430f2e9fb6e2d73d: Section 52 stipulates that an abettor is liable to punishment for each of the offences if the act for which they are liable under section 51 is committed in addition to the act abetted and constitutes a distinct offence. The section includes an illustration wherein an abettor instigating the resistance of a public servant's distress is liable for both the resistance and the voluntarily causing of grievous hurt, provided the abettor knew such hurt was likely.
BNS_S54:87dec607f73162737350fdb806f50284: Section 54 addresses the liability of an abettor present when an offence is committed. Whenever any person who would be liable to be punished as an abettor if absent is present when the act or offence for which he would be punishable in consequence of the abetment is committed, he shall be deemed to have committed such act or offence.
BNS_S56:3b4f9a81501c496ad01e90ba515b261b: Section 56 provides that whoever abets an offence punishable with imprisonment, where the offence is not committed and no express provision is made under this Sanhita for the punishment of such abetment, shall be punished with imprisonment of any description for a term which may extend to one-fourth part of the longest term provided for that offence, or with such fine as is provided for that offence, or with both. If the abettor or the person abetted is a public servant, whose duty it is to prevent the commission of such offence, the abettor shall be punished with imprisonment of any description for a term which may extend to one-half of the longest term provided for that offence, or with such fine as is provided for the offence, or with both. The section includes Illustrations concerning the abetment of false evidence and robbery.
BNS_S33:b710e5bbd64d8f1683899d186249a8bb: Section 33, "Act causing slight harm," stipulates that nothing is an offence by reason that it causes, or that it is intended to cause, or that it is known to be likely to cause, any harm. This exception applies if the harm is so slight that no person of ordinary sense and temper would complain of such harm. The provided text also includes the heading "Of right of private defence."
BNS_S58:42262004e249e6c3dc874e5f587e8ac5: Section 58 penalizes whoever, intending to facilitate or knowing it to be likely that they will facilitate the commission of an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, voluntarily conceals the design of such offence through any act, omission, encryption, or any other information hiding tool, or makes a false representation respecting such design. If the offence is committed, the actor is punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years; if the offence is not committed, the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, and they shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S35:aa503c15c24b816c8cc20f369ed06d00: Subject to the restrictions contained in section 37, every person has a right to defend his own body and the body of any other person against any offence affecting the human body. This right also extends to the defense of property, whether movable or immovable, of himself or of any other person against any act which is an offence falling under the definition of theft, robbery, mischief, or criminal trespass, or an attempt to commit theft, robbery, mischief, or criminal trespass.
BNS_S64:8167036c281ec2614b707c53e83f923b: Section 64(1) mandates that whoever commits rape shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than ten years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine. Section 64(2) prescribes rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than ten years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life (meaning imprisonment for the remainder of that person’s natural life) and a fine, if the offence is committed by a police officer (within station limits, station house, or on a woman in custody), a public servant (on a woman in custody), armed forces member (in deployed area), management/staff of a jail, remand home, other place of custody, women’s or children’s institution (on an inmate), hospital management/staff, or a relative, guardian, teacher, or person in trust, authority, control, or dominance. This same penalty applies to rape during communal or sectarian violence, on a woman known to be pregnant, a woman incapable of giving consent, a woman with mental or physical disability, repeated rape on the same woman, or rape causing grievous bodily harm, maiming, disfiguring, or endangering life. An Explanation provides specific definitions for “armed forces,” “hospital,” “police officer,” and “women’s or children’s institution.”
BNS_S72:addffd2967e41fc68f6cfd309a6c9fc3: Whoever prints or publishes the name or identity of a victim of offences under section 64, 65, 66, 67, 68, 69, 70, or 71 shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years and shall also be liable to fine. Sub-section (2) exempts publications authorized in writing by the victim, or by the next of kin (if the victim is dead, a child, or of unsound mind), or by the officer-in-charge of the police station or investigating police officer acting in good faith for investigation purposes. A Proviso limits next of kin authorisation to the chairman or secretary of a “recognised welfare institution or organisation,” which the Explanation defines as a social welfare institution or organisation recognised by the Central Government or the State Government.
BNS_S76:4bbb20367dd2f28fc052f3049db2c4d1: Section 76 criminalizes the act of whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any woman, or abets such act, with the intention of disrobing or compelling her to be naked. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than three years but which may extend to seven years, and the perpetrator shall also be liable to fine. This section contains no Provisos, Exceptions, or Explanations.
BNS_S43:6459764d5d31548fb5d3322a9de525a1: The right of private defence of property commences when a reasonable apprehension of danger to the property commences. Against theft, it continues till the offender has effected his retreat with the property or either the assistance of the public authorities is obtained, or the property has been recovered; against robbery, it continues as long as the offender causes or attempts to cause to any person death or hurt or wrongful restraint, or as long as the fear of instant death or of instant hurt or of instant personal restraint continues. Against criminal trespass or mischief, it continues as long as the offender continues in the commission of criminal trespass or mischief, and against house-breaking after sunset and before sunrise, it continues as long as the house-trespass which has been begun by such house-breaking continues.
BNS_S186:110b4493d66e23a0c88167f0fa6333f: Section 186(1) prohibits making, knowingly uttering, dealing in, selling, or knowingly using for any postal purpose any "fictitious stamp," as well as the possession of a "fictitious stamp" or any die, plate, instrument, or materials for making one without lawful excuse, punishable by a fine which may extend to two hundred rupees. Section 186(2) provides that any such stamp, die, plate, instrument, or materials may be seized and, if seized, shall be forfeited. Section 186(3) defines "fictitious stamp" as any stamp falsely purporting to be issued by Government for denoting a rate of postage, or any facsimile, imitation, or representation, whether on paper or otherwise. Section 186(4) specifies that "Government" includes any person or persons authorised by law to administer executive Government in any part of India or any foreign country for the purposes of this section and sections 178 to 181 and 183 to 185.
BNS_S45:cd6f56c43fb11d9152bec2563c03e99d: A person abets the doing of a thing by (a) instigating any person to do that thing; (b) engaging with one or more other person or persons in any conspiracy for the doing of that thing, if an act or illegal omission takes place in pursuance of that conspiracy and in order to the doing of that thing; or (c) intentionally aiding, by any act or illegal omission, the doing of that thing. Explanation 1 states that a person is said to instigate the doing of a thing if they, by wilful misrepresentation or by wilful concealment of a material fact which he is bound to disclose, voluntarily cause or procure, or attempt to cause or procure, a thing to be done. Explanation 2 provides that whoever, either prior to or at the time of the commission of an act, does anything in order to facilitate the commission of that act and thereby facilitates the commission thereof, is said to aid the doing of that act.
BNS_S196:5e6c27b2c58660b954526b1119d170a: Section 196 (1) penalizes promoting or attempting to promote disharmony, feelings of enmity, hatred or ill-will between different religious, racial, language or regional groups or castes or communities via words (spoken or written), signs, visible representations, electronic communication, or otherwise, on grounds of religion, race, place of birth, residence, language, caste or community or any other ground whatsoever. It further criminalizes acts prejudicial to the maintenance of harmony that disturb or are likely to disturb public tranquillity, as well as organizing or participating in exercises, movements, drills, or similar activities involving criminal force or violence that cause or are likely to cause fear, alarm or a feeling of insecurity among such groups. Such offenses are punishable with imprisonment which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both. Under Section 196 (2), if these offenses are committed in any place of worship or in any assembly engaged in the performance of religious worship or religious ceremonies, the punishment is imprisonment which may extend to five years and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S49:32318936784521b439f0f600d321d60e: Section 49 provides that whoever abets any offence shall, if the act abetted is committed in consequence of the abetment and no express provision is made by this Sanhita for the punishment of such abetment, be punished with the punishment provided for the offence. The Explanation clarifies that an act or offence is said to be committed in consequence of abetment when it is committed in consequence of the instigation, or in pursuance of the conspiracy, or with the aid which constitutes the abetment.
BNS_S198:5d5e90042106effd99a8d19b445db39: Section 198 stipulates that whoever, being a public servant, knowingly disobeys any direction of the law regarding the conduct of their official duties, intending to cause or knowing it likely that such disobedience will cause injury to any person, commits an offence. The punishment is simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both. The section includes an illustration regarding an officer knowingly disobeying directions to take property in execution to satisfy a court decree.
BNS_S200:56f6ee94ef552b8432ee826ea5fb609: Section 200 prescribes the punishment for non-treatment of victim. It applies to whoever, being in charge of a hospital, public or private, whether run by the Central Government, the State Government, local bodies or any other person, contravenes the provisions of section 397 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023. Such contravention shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S202:7bd73a3121b447d749f63dff1621c49: Section 202 penalizes any public servant who, being legally bound as such public servant not to engage in trade, engages in trade. The prescribed punishment is simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both or with community service. This section contains no provisos, exceptions, or explanations.
BNS_S206:4f9ff11b37f3eb13b117d7a0fe7dcf0: Section 206 penalizes whoever absconds to avoid being served with a summons, notice or order proceeding from any public servant legally competent to issue such summons, notice or order. General absconding is punishable with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both. Where the summons, notice or order is to attend in person or by agent, or to produce a document or an electronic record in a Court, the punishment is simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S55:e70f8d2edfd849460b6d8745437072a0: Whoever abets the commission of an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, if that offence be not committed in consequence of the abetment and no express provision is made under this Sanhita for the punishment of such abetment, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine. If any act for which the abettor is liable in consequence of the abetment, and which causes hurt to any person, is done, the abettor shall be liable to imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to fourteen years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S208:d21ca591614f4d2a15216ab36d4c999: Section 208 penalizes whoever, being legally bound to attend in person or by an agent at a certain place and time in obedience to a summons, notice, order, or proclamation from a legally competent public servant, intentionally omits to attend or departs before the lawful time. General non-attendance is punishable with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both. Where the summons, notice, order or proclamation is to attend in person or by agent in a Court, the punishment is simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both. The section includes Illustrations regarding non-attendance before a High Court via subpoena and before a District Judge as a witness.
BNS_S212:a71d8973149ea613462acdc8845ff56: Section 212 penalizes whoever, being legally bound to furnish information on any subject to any public servant, furnishes as true information which he knows or has reason to believe to be false. For general subjects, the punishment is simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both. Where the information respects the commission of an offence, the prevention of an offence, or the apprehension of an offender, the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. An Explanation specifies that "offence" includes acts committed outside India punishable under sections 103, 105, 307, sub-sections (2), (3), and (4) of section 309, sub-sections (2), (3), (4), and (5) of section 310, 311, 312, clauses (f) and (g) of section 326, sub-sections (4), (6), (7), and (8) of section 331, and clauses (a) and (b) of section 332, and "offender" includes any person alleged to have been guilty of such acts.
BNS_S214:7806dc7102956cf0353a148c2e6f130: Section 214 penalizes whoever, being legally bound to state the truth on any subject to any public servant, refuses to answer any question demanded of him touching that subject by such public servant in the exercise of the legal powers of such public servant. The offence is punishable with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S216:4385d5b6dbbee69357451beb93c8225: Section 216 penalizes whoever, being legally bound by an oath or affirmation to state the truth on any subject to any public servant or other person authorised by law to administer such oath or affirmation, makes a statement touching that subject which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true. Such person shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, and shall also be liable to fine. This section contains no Provisos, Exceptions, or Explanations.
BNS_S59:4ac0634d96556297466c6da05ebcbd39: A public servant who, intending to facilitate or knowing it is likely to facilitate the commission of an offence which it is his duty to prevent, voluntarily conceals a design to commit such offence through any act, omission, use of encryption, or any other information hiding tool, or makes any representation known to be false, is subject to specific penalties. If the offence is committed, the punishment is imprisonment of any description provided for the offence for a term which may extend to one-half of the longest term of such imprisonment, or such fine as is provided for that offence, or both; or if the offence is punishable with death or imprisonment for life, the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years. If the offence is not committed, the punishment is imprisonment of any description provided for the offence for a term which may extend to one-fourth part of the longest term of such imprisonment or such fine as is provided for the offence, or both.
BNS_S224:2218cb03a33a4bd4bb8c941f0f0f781: Section 224 penalizes whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom he believes that public servant to be interested. The offence requires the threat be made for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant. The punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S226:eaba77e7314563edc20cab1ea1ce80c: Section 226 penalizes whoever attempts to commit suicide with the intent to compel or restrain any public servant from discharging his official duty. The prescribed punishment is simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both, or with community service.
BNS_S63:c106d9a80b6cbf4191ab6a67d36f05ad: A man commits “rape” by penetrating his penis into the vagina, mouth, urethra, or anus of a woman, inserting any object or a part of the body, not being the penis, into the vagina, urethra, or anus, manipulating any part of the body of a woman to cause penetration into the vagina, urethra, anus or any part of body, or applying his mouth to the vagina, anus, or urethra, or making her do so with him or any other person, under seven descriptions: against her will, without her consent, consent via fear of death or of hurt, consent via mistaken identity of husband, consent due to unsoundness of mind, intoxication, or administration of stupefying or unwholesome substances, when she is under eighteen years of age, or when she is unable to communicate consent. Explanation 1 clarifies that “vagina” includes labia majora, and Explanation 2 defines “consent” as an unequivocal voluntary agreement via words, gestures, or verbal/non-verbal communication, subject to a Proviso that a woman who does not physically resist shall not be regarded as consenting solely by that fact. Exception 1 excludes medical procedures or interventions, and Exception 2 excludes sexual intercourse or sexual acts by a man with his own wife, provided the wife is not under eighteen years of age.
BNS_S228:472c10be486efe8ff52768afbcf695e: Section 228 defines “fabricating false evidence” as causing any circumstance to exist, making a false entry in any book, record, or electronic record, or creating any document or electronic record containing a false statement. This act must be committed with the intent that such circumstance, false entry, or false statement appears in evidence in a judicial proceeding, a proceeding taken by law before a public servant as such, or before an arbitrator. The objective must be to cause any person forming an opinion upon the evidence to entertain an erroneous opinion touching any point material to the result of such proceeding. The section includes Illustrations to clarify these applications.
BNS_S230:e7baaa4d4b312dfa3a1ec44872f9d7c: Section 230(1) stipulates that whoever gives or fabricates false evidence, intending or knowing it likely to cause a person to be convicted of an offence that is capital by the law for the time being in force in India, shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and a fine which may extend to fifty thousand rupees. Under sub-section (2), if an innocent person is convicted and executed in consequence of such false evidence, the perpetrator shall be punished with death or the punishment specified in sub-section (1).
BNS_S65:3eec209bd1353b085d8bae735a476279: Section 65(1) stipulates that whoever commits rape on a woman under sixteen years of age shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than twenty years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person’s natural life, and shall also be liable to fine, subject to two provisos stating that such fine shall be just and reasonable to meet the medical expenses and rehabilitation of the victim and shall be paid to the victim. Section 65(2) mandates that whoever commits rape on a woman under twelve years of age shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than twenty years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person’s natural life, and with fine or with death. Sub-section (2) also contains two provisos requiring that any fine imposed shall be just and reasonable to meet the medical expenses and rehabilitation of the victim and shall be paid to the victim.
BNS_S232:b76eeb97e7e9844021f537c9d6fb9cb: Section 232 stipulates that whoever threatens another with injury to their person, reputation, or property, or to the person or reputation of any one in whom that person is interested, with intent to cause that person to give false evidence, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both. Per sub-section (2), if an innocent person is convicted and sentenced to death or imprisonment for more than seven years in consequence of such false evidence, the person who threatens shall be punished with the same punishment and sentence in the same manner and to the same extent as the innocent person.
BNS_S67:9c0757f40045e82b712972a732d75776: Whoever has sexual intercourse with his own wife, who is living separately, whether under a decree of separation or otherwise, without her consent, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than two years but which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine. The section includes an Explanation stating that “sexual intercourse” shall mean any of the acts mentioned in clauses (a) to (d) of section 63.
BNS_S234:1be731003f4fc6b828d623af9a4174c: Section 234 penalizes whoever issues or signs any certificate required by law to be given or signed, or relating to any fact of which such certificate is by law admissible in evidence, while knowing or believing that such certificate is false in any material point. Such person shall be punished in the same manner as if he gave false evidence.
BNS_S69:5f499b6783a85cd65c42b287a13427bb: Section 69 stipulates that whoever, by deceitful means or by making promise to marry to a woman without any intention of fulfilling the same, has sexual intercourse with her, such sexual intercourse not amounting to the offence of rape, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years and shall also be liable to fine. The Explanation clarifies that “deceitful means” shall include inducement for, or false promise of employment or promotion, or marrying by suppressing identity.
BNS_S240:5d38df3b7925c305490fc9d63ec4dcd: Section 240 provides that whoever, knowing or having reason to believe an offence has been committed, gives information respecting that offence which they know or believe to be false, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. An Explanation clarifies that in sections 238, 239, and 240, the word “offence” includes any act committed outside India which, if committed in India, would be punishable under sections 103, 105, 307, sub-sections (2), (3) and (4) of section 309, sub-sections (2), (3), (4) and (5) of section 310, 311, 312, clauses (f) and (g) of section 326, sub-sections (4), (6), (7) and (8) of section 331, and clauses (a) and (b) of section 332.
BNS_S71:fa32f757c608a0cdd39c47f2524a9db5: Section 71 prescribes punishment for repeat offenders who have been previously convicted of an offence punishable under section 64 or section 65 or section 66 or section 70 and are subsequently convicted of an offence punishable under any of the said sections. Such persons shall be punished with imprisonment for life, which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person’s natural life, or with death.
BNS_S242:8c41881ca38be39003af2cbdc8c0642: Section 242 penalizes whoever falsely personates another to make any admission or statement, confess judgment, cause any process to be issued, become bail or security, or perform any other act in any suit or criminal prosecution. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S244:2e84b18aa7e33cb1200eab95b93ddcb: Section 244 criminalizes whoever fraudulently accepts, receives or claims any property or any interest therein, knowing they have no right or rightful claim, or practises any deception touching any right to any property or any interest therein. The act must be intended to prevent such property or interest from being taken as a forfeiture or in satisfaction of a fine under a sentence pronounced or likely to be pronounced by a Court or other competent authority, or from being taken in execution of a decree or order made or likely to be made by a Court in a civil suit. The punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S246:311aaf95d65ba3d65747fc6b325ee20: Section 246 penalizes whoever fraudulently or dishonestly, or with intent to injure or annoy any person, makes in a Court any claim which he knows to be false. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, and the perpetrator shall also be liable to fine. This section contains no Provisos, Exceptions, or Explanations.
BNS_S75:5eab04b9e1eb77149ea004395a8e0b7f: A man is guilty of the offence of sexual harassment if he commits physical contact and advances involving unwelcome and explicit sexual overtures, a demand or request for sexual favours, showing pornography against the will of a woman, or making sexually coloured remarks. For acts specified in clauses (i), (ii), or (iii), the punishment is rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both. For the offence specified in clause (iv), the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S248:9710c2e77c39e4e202ed2abfe258984: Section 248 penalizes whoever, with intent to cause injury, institutes or causes to be instituted any criminal proceeding or falsely charges a person with having committed an offence, knowing there is no just or lawful ground for such proceeding or charge. The general punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, or with fine which may extend to two lakh rupees, or with both. If the criminal proceeding is instituted on a false charge of an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life, or imprisonment for ten years or upwards, the actor shall be punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S250:3839f4eb17b52f934b78a1b22377218: Section 250 penalizes whoever accepts, attempts to obtain, or agrees to accept any gratification or restitution of property for themselves or any other person in consideration of concealing an offence, screening any person from legal punishment, or not proceeding against any person to bring them to legal punishment. If the offence is punishable with death, the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years and a fine; if punishable with imprisonment for life or imprisonment which may extend to ten years, the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years and a fine. For offences punishable with imprisonment not extending to ten years, the penalty is imprisonment of the description provided for the offence for a term which may extend to one-fourth part of the longest term of imprisonment provided for the offence, or fine, or both.
BNS_S252:96a864208b0d536bbeee4ffb601484c: Section 252 stipulates that whoever takes, agrees, or consents to take any gratification under pretence or on account of helping any person recover movable property of which he shall have been deprived by any offence punishable under this Sanhita is liable for punishment. This penalty applies unless the person uses all means in his power to cause the offender to be apprehended and convicted of the offence. The punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S254:ac7e21c710da64aa07f452f053b53e6: Section 254 penalizes whoever, knowing or having reason to believe that persons are about to commit or have recently committed robbery or dacoity, harbours them or any of them with the intention of facilitating the commission of such robbery or dacoity or screening them from punishment. The offence is punishable with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine. An Explanation specifies that it is immaterial whether the robbery or dacoity is intended to be committed, or has been committed, within or without India. An Exception stipulates that these provisions do not extend to the case in which the harbour is by the spouse of the offender.
BNS_S77:ebe07e471547f5e5c128ab435423a164: Section 77 defines voyeurism as watching, capturing the image of a woman engaging in a "private act" where she would usually have the expectation of not being observed either by the perpetrator or by any other person at the behest of the perpetrator, or disseminating such an image. On first conviction, the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than one year, but which may extend to three years, and shall also be liable to fine, while a second or subsequent conviction is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than three years, but which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine. Explanation 1 clarifies that "private act" includes watching in a place reasonably expected to provide privacy where the victim’s genitals, posterior, or breasts are exposed or covered only in underwear, the use of a lavatory, or performing a sexual act not ordinarily done in public. Explanation 2 states that where a victim consents to the capture of images or any act, but not to their dissemination to third persons, such dissemination shall be considered an offence under this section.
BNS_S256:2956bbf3e6881c6bb473376a7d5e690: Section 256 penalizes a public servant, charged with the preparation of any record or other writing, who frames such record or writing in a manner he knows to be incorrect. This action must be committed with intent to cause, or knowing it to be likely to cause, loss or injury to the public or any person, or to save any person from legal punishment, or to save any property from forfeiture or other charge to which it is liable by law. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S266:4cf6e62b82253be3e8850b1d6add581: Section 266 stipulates that whoever, having accepted any conditional remission of punishment, knowingly violates any condition on which such remission was granted, shall be punished. If the offender has already suffered no part of that punishment, they shall be punished with the punishment to which he was originally sentenced. If they have suffered any part of that punishment, they shall be punished with so much of that punishment as he has not already suffered.
BNS_S79:0c52ee74f3f698b87793244b84ccaf2f: Section 79 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, addresses word, gesture, or act intended to insult the modesty of a woman. It penalizes whoever, intending to insult the modesty of any woman, utters any words, makes any sound or gesture, or exhibits any object in any form, intending that such word or sound shall be heard, or that such gesture or object shall be seen, by such woman, or intrudes upon the privacy of such woman. Such an individual shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years, and also with fine.
BNS_S268:4eb116af7fdcf6704f4be38af7d5609: Section 268 penalizes the "Personation of assessor," applicable to whoever, by personation or otherwise, intentionally causes or knowingly suffers himself to be returned, empanelled or sworn as an assessor in any case where he knows he is not entitled by law to be so returned, empanelled or sworn. It further prohibits anyone, knowing himself to have been so returned, empanelled or sworn contrary to law, from voluntarily serving as such assessor. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S270:ae03a6024b167c0bc524ef84dda4ec1: Section 270 defines "Public nuisance" as any act or illegal omission by a person that causes common injury, danger, or annoyance to the public or people who dwell or occupy property in the vicinity. This offence also includes acts that must necessarily cause injury, obstruction, danger, or annoyance to persons who may have occasion to use any public right. A common nuisance is not excused on the ground that it causes some convenience or advantage. This section contains no Provisos, Exceptions, or Explanations.
BNS_S272:af5ba15f27c1fd6dbc51284e2c6c8a3: Section 272 penalizes whoever malignantly does any act which is, and which he knows or has reason to believe to be, likely to spread the infection of any disease dangerous to life. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. This section contains no Provisos, Exceptions, or Explanations.
BNS_S81:8133bd8068c8dc265a967df69604120c: Section 81 addresses every man who, by deceit, causes any woman who is not lawfully married to him to believe that she is lawfully married to him and to cohabit or have sexual intercourse with him in that belief. The offender shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S278:cae809d3f0c109f29bddd7a39347244: Section 278 penalizes whoever knowingly sells, offers or exposes for sale, or issues from a dispensary for medicinal purposes, any drug or medical preparation as a different drug or medical preparation. The punishment for this offence is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S280:06b6d88ab407e8efef56c1ba2a585fb: Section 280 penalizes whoever voluntarily vitiates the atmosphere in any place so as to make it noxious to the health of persons in general dwelling or carrying on business in the neighbourhood or passing along a public way. The offence is punishable with a fine which may extend to one thousand rupees. This section contains no Provisos, Exceptions, or Explanations.
BNS_S83:6c9a03602e3cca319d06143d640a38fe: Section 83 addresses a marriage ceremony fraudulently gone through without lawful marriage. It mandates that whoever, dishonestly or with a fraudulent intention, goes through the ceremony of being married, knowing that he is not thereby lawfully married, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S284:439658c0e7fcc4409d3b2c451603251: Section 284 penalizes whoever knowingly or negligently conveys, or causes to be conveyed for hire, any person by water in any vessel that is in such a state or so loaded as to endanger the life of that person. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S286:71dcbb628cee4654ff695dec7a96600: Section 286 penalizes whoever performs any act with any poisonous substance in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any person. It similarly applies to whoever knowingly or negligently omits to take such order with any poisonous substance in his possession as is sufficient to guard against any probable danger to human life from such poisonous substance. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S85:706137faa3ab2073b2ef48977d3275b2: Under Section 85 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, whoever, being the husband or the relative of the husband of a woman, subjects such woman to cruelty shall be punished. The prescribed punishment is imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S288:17ebb5976cdfb9e09517555e0534953: Section 288 penalizes whoever does any act with any explosive substance so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person. It also applies to whoever knowingly or negligently omits to take such order with any explosive substance in his possession as is sufficient to guard against any probable danger to human life from that substance. The punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S87:ec5cf5bd6da7897ea3de754fea8c24f0: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any woman with intent that she may be compelled, or knowing it to be likely that she will be compelled, to marry any person against her will, or in order that she may be forced or seduced to illicit intercourse, or knowing it to be likely that she will be forced or seduced to illicit intercourse, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine. Furthermore, whoever induces any woman to go from any place by means of criminal intimidation as defined in this Sanhita, abuse of authority, or any other method of compulsion, with intent that she may be, or knowing that it is likely that she will be, forced or seduced to illicit intercourse with another person, shall also be punishable as aforesaid.
BNS_S290:10fa6511db82f7c77a760b1d8034930: Section 290 penalizes whoever, in pulling down, repairing or constructing any building, knowingly or negligently omits to take such measures as is sufficient to guard against any probable danger to human life from the fall of that building, or of any part thereof. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both. This section contains no Provisos, Exceptions, or Explanations.
BNS_S292:4bc67d0578ba85fb3247c0cb8321e13: Section 292 prescribes the punishment for whoever commits a public nuisance in any case not otherwise punishable by this Sanhita. The offence is punishable with a fine which may extend to one thousand rupees.
BNS_S91:138c19fd67fb891d55cd986ac11a9a24: Whoever, before the birth of any child, does any act with the intention of preventing that child from being born alive or causing it to die after its birth, and does by such act prevent that child from being born alive or causes it to die after its birth, commits the offence. This section contains an exception for acts done in good faith for the purpose of saving the life of the mother. Punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S294:2d8b2b3197eae7fbb60dd4dd93f1509: Section 294 deems any book, pamphlet, paper, writing, drawing, painting, representation, figure, or other object, including content in electronic form, as "obscene" if it is lascivious, appeals to the prurient interest, or tends to deprave and corrupt persons likely to read, see, or hear it. It criminalizes selling, letting to hire, distributing, publicly exhibiting, circulating, making, producing, possessing, importing, exporting, conveying, profiting from businesses dealing in, advertising, offering, or attempting to perform these acts involving obscene objects. First conviction is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years and fine which may extend to five thousand rupees; second or subsequent convictions carry imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years and fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees. An Exception excludes objects justified for the public good (science, literature, art, learning, or general concern), those kept bona fide for religious purposes, and representations on temples, cars used for idol conveyance, or ancient monuments under the Ancient Monuments and Archaeological Sites and Remains Act, 1958.
BNS_S95:af91e1520a75b674014a70b0025f367f: Whoever hires, employs or engages any child to commit an offence shall be punished with imprisonment of either description which shall not be less than three years but which may extend to ten years, and with fine. If the offence be committed, the individual shall also be punished with the punishment provided for that offence as if the offence has been committed by such person himself. The Explanation clarifies that hiring, employing, engaging or using a child for sexual exploitation or pornography is covered within the meaning of this section.
BNS_S296:5f939c80bf1335b307caea4630237b4: Section 296 prescribes punishment for whoever, to the annoyance of others, does any obscene act in any public place or sings, recites or utters any obscene song, ballad or words, in or near any public place. The offense is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S298:066729d4330ef477b526fac5a178a04: Section 298 penalizes whoever destroys, damages, or defiles any place of worship or any object held sacred by any class of persons. The offence requires the intention of insulting the religion of any class of persons or the knowledge that any class of persons is likely to consider such destruction, damage, or defilement as an insult to their religion. Punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S97:a34eecbed21b5b397e10242d53a05486: Under Section 97, whoever kidnaps or abducts any child under the age of ten years with the intention of taking dishonestly any movable property from the person of such child shall be punished. The offender shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S300:4c7463a888e0dac642da726aca66d13: Section 300 (Disturbing religious assembly) stipulates that whoever voluntarily causes disturbance to any assembly lawfully engaged in the performance of religious worship or religious ceremonies shall be punished. The prescribed penalty is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S302:ac35554b134f67d88ad54dc8cebf3fd: Section 302 criminalizes the act of whoever, with the deliberate intention of wounding the religious feelings of any person, utters any word, makes any sound in the hearing of that person, makes any gesture in the sight of that person, or places any object in the sight of that person. This offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both. This section contains no Provisos, Exceptions, or Explanations.
BNS_S304:97f19ecb24afa8aa1790595e9a9e469: Section 304(1) defines theft as snatching if the offender suddenly or quickly or forcibly seizes or secures or grabs or takes away any movable property from any person or from his possession. Under Section 304(2), whoever commits snatching shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S99:76aaf6dfa6034a0e46ac6ce03168bc6b: Whoever buys, hires or otherwise obtains possession of any child with intent that such child shall at any age be employed or used for prostitution, illicit intercourse with any person, or any unlawful and immoral purpose, or knowing it to be likely that such child will at any age be employed or used for any such purpose, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than seven years but which may extend to fourteen years, and shall also be liable to fine. Explanation 1 stipulates that any prostitute or person keeping or managing a brothel who obtains possession of a female under the age of eighteen years shall, until the contrary is proved, be presumed to have obtained such possession with the intent that she shall be used for the purpose of prostitution. Explanation 2 clarifies that “illicit intercourse” has the same meaning as in section 98.
BNS_S308:3ca5065161ae492a4571696c8e39b00: Section 308(1) defines extortion as intentionally putting any person in fear of injury to that person or any other to dishonestly induce the delivery of property, valuable security, or anything signed or sealed convertible into a valuable security. General extortion is punishable by imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or fine, or both [Section 308(2)], while putting or attempting to put a person in fear of injury to commit extortion is punishable by imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or fine, or both [Section 308(3)]. Putting or attempting to put a person in fear of death or grievous hurt to commit extortion carries imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years and fine [Section 308(4)], while committing extortion through such fear is punishable by imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years and fine [Section 308(5)]. Putting or attempting to put a person in fear of an accusation of an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life, or imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years—or attempting to induce another to commit such offence—to commit or commit extortion is punishable by imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years and fine [Section 308(6) and (7)].
BNS_S101:6d897096953d67f31ba37be66c8ff31: Section 101 defines murder as culpable homicide committed (a) with the intention of causing death; (b) with the intention of causing bodily injury the offender knows is likely to cause death to the person harmed; (c) with the intention of causing bodily injury sufficient in the ordinary course of nature to cause death; or (d) through an act so imminently dangerous that it must, in all probability, cause death or likely bodily injury, committed without excuse. The section provides five Exceptions: Exception 1 (deprivation of self-control by grave and sudden provocation causing death by mistake or accident, subject to a Proviso regarding provocation sought by the offender, given in obedience to law or by a public servant, or via lawful private defence, and an Explanation that it is a question of fact); Exception 2 (exceeding the right of private defence of person or property in good faith without premeditation or unnecessary harm); Exception 3 (public servants exceeding powers in good faith for duty discharge without ill-will); Exception 4 (sudden fight in the heat of passion without premeditation, undue advantage, or cruel/unusual manner, including an Explanation that the initial aggressor is immaterial); and Exception 5 (death or risk of death with consent by a person above the age of eighteen years).
BNS_S310:bc0a98d89c05abe1932ff6a68fb2d43: Section 310 defines dacoity as occurring when five or more persons conjointly commit or attempt a robbery, or where the total number of persons committing, attempting, and present and aiding amounts to five or more. Dacoity is punishable by imprisonment for life, or rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and fine; however, if any person commits murder during dacoity, every person involved is punishable by death, imprisonment for life, or rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than ten years, and fine. Preparation for committing dacoity is punishable by rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years and fine, while assembly of five or more persons for the purpose of committing dacoity is punishable by rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years and fine. Belonging to a gang associated for habitually committing dacoity is punishable by imprisonment for life, or rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and fine.
BNS_S312:08687dd9b2b94089443be1acf3cf643: Section 312 prescribes the punishment for the attempt to commit robbery or dacoity when armed with deadly weapon. If the offender is armed with any deadly weapon at the time of the attempt, the imprisonment shall not be less than seven years.
BNS_S103:db35bace78904c227f20f6c8c30bb97: Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine. When a group of five or more persons acting in concert commits murder on the ground of race, caste or community, sex, place of birth, language, personal belief or any other similar ground, each member of such group shall be punished with death or with imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S314:499f20b57898dc75525ca18918559a6: Section 314 provides that whoever dishonestly misappropriates or converts to his own use any movable property shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than six months but which may extend to two years and with fine. Explanation 1 clarifies that a dishonest misappropriation for a time only is a misappropriation within the meaning of this section. Explanation 2 states that taking property not in the possession of any other person to protect or restore it to the owner is not dishonest; however, the finder is guilty if they appropriate it to their own use while knowing or having the means of discovering the owner, or before using reasonable means to discover and give notice to the owner and keeping the property a reasonable time (which is a question of fact). Under Explanation 2, it is sufficient if the finder, at the time of appropriating, does not believe the property to be his own or does not in good faith believe that the real owner cannot be found.
BNS_S105:6e4eb57f3caefa29d6e480f1abda196: Whoever commits culpable homicide not amounting to murder with the intention of causing death, or of causing such bodily injury as is likely to cause death, shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than five years but which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine. If the act is done with the knowledge that it is likely to cause death, but without any intention to cause death, or to cause such bodily injury as is likely to cause death, the punishment shall be imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years and with fine.
BNS_S316:c1c520945eba586da96832f779bc991: Section 316 defines "Criminal breach of trust" as the act of whoever, being entrusted with property or dominion over property, dishonestly misappropriates, converts to own use, or disposes of that property in violation of any direction of law or any express or implied legal contract, or wilfully suffers any other person so to do. Explanation 1 and Explanation 2 specify that employers who deduct employee contributions for a Provident Fund or Family Pension Fund (under the Employees’ Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952) or the Employees’ State Insurance Fund (under the Employees’ State Insurance Act, 1948) and default in payment are deemed to have been entrusted with and dishonestly used such contributions. General commission of this offence is punishable by imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, or with fine, or with both; however, carriers, wharfingers, warehouse-keepers, clerks, or servants face imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years and shall also be liable to fine. If committed by a public servant or in the capacity of a banker, merchant, factor, broker, attorney, or agent, the punishment is imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S107:03824aaf48b1158af574ab2d3950523: Section 107 stipulates punishment for whoever abets the commission of suicide by any child, any person of unsound mind, any delirious person, or any person in a state of intoxication. Such an individual shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life, or imprisonment for a term not exceeding ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S318:cbd1368686b9f61ba7304b21a0c4ae4: Section 318 defines cheating as deceiving a person to fraudulently or dishonestly induce the delivery or retention of property, or intentionally inducing an act or omission likely to cause damage or harm to that person in body, mind, reputation, or property, including an Explanation that dishonest concealment of facts constitutes deception. General cheating is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both. Cheating involving a person whose interest the offender was bound by law or legal contract to protect is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, or with fine, or with both. Cheating that dishonestly induces the delivery of property or the making, altering, or destroying of a valuable security, or anything capable of being converted into a valuable security, is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S322:c4374a490e37f114768fc9d41728242: Section 322 criminalizes the act of whoever dishonestly or fraudulently signs, executes, or becomes a party to any deed or instrument purporting to transfer or subject to any charge any property, or any interest therein. The offence occurs if the instrument contains any false statement relating to the consideration for such transfer or charge, or the person or persons for whose use or benefit it is really intended to operate. Punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S324:47f9da70527dfeccbaeceeb06244b76: Section 324 defines mischief as causing destruction of any property, or any change in property or its situation that destroys or diminishes its value or utility, or affects it injuriously, with intent to cause or knowing it is likely to cause wrongful loss or damage to the public or any person. Explanation 1 clarifies that intent to cause loss to the owner is not essential if there is intent or knowledge of wrongful loss or damage to any person, and Explanation 2 states mischief may involve property belonging to the offender alone or jointly. General mischief is punishable by imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or fine, or both, while mischief causing loss or damage to property including Government or Local Authority property is punishable by imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or fine, or both. Specific thresholds for loss or damage carry punishments of imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or fine, or both (for twenty thousand rupees and more but less than one lakh rupees), or a term which may extend to five years, or fine, or both (for one lakh rupees or upwards), while mischief with preparation for death, hurt, wrongful restraint, or fear thereof is punishable by imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S111:8d0fe5f6898ec974648d48f4753537f: Section 111 defines organised crime as any continuing unlawful activity—including kidnapping, robbery, vehicle theft, extortion, land grabbing, contract killing, economic offence, cyber-crimes, trafficking of persons, drugs, weapons or illicit goods or services, or human trafficking for prostitution or ransom—committed by a person or group acting singly or jointly as a member of or on behalf of an organised crime syndicate (two or more persons) through violence, threat of violence, intimidation, coercion, or other unlawful means to obtain direct or indirect material or financial benefit. Explanations define "continuing unlawful activity" as a cognizable offence punishable with imprisonment of three years or more involving more than one charge-sheets filed before a competent Court within the preceding period of ten years where cognizance is taken, and "economic offence" as including criminal breach of trust, forgery, counterfeiting of currency-notes, bank-notes and Government stamps, hawala transaction, mass-marketing fraud, or defrauding persons or institutions for monetary benefits. Committing organised crime resulting in death is punishable with death or imprisonment for life and a fine not less than ten lakh rupees; however, in any other case, abetment, conspiracy, facilitation, or membership in a syndicate is punishable with imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than five years but which may extend to imprisonment for life and a fine not less than five lakh rupees. Harbouring or concealing carries imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than three years but which may extend to imprisonment for life and a fine not less than five lakh rupees (subject to a Proviso exempting the spouse of the offender), possession of proceeds of organised crime carries imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than three years but which may extend to imprisonment for life and a fine not less than two lakh rupees, and possessing unaccounted property on behalf of a syndicate member carries imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than three years but which may extend to imprisonment for ten years and a fine not less than one lakh rupees.
BNS_S326:e99fcfe5072aa7100f9e00b9bc5cb99: Section 326 prescribes punishments for mischief committed via acts causing diminution of water supply for agriculture, humans, animals, cleanliness, or manufacture; rendering public roads, bridges, or navigable rivers or channels (natural or artificial) impassable or less safe; or causing inundation or obstruction to public drainage attended with injury or damage, each punishable by imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, or with fine, or with both. Destroying, moving, or rendering less useful signs or signals for rail, aircraft, ships, or guides for navigators is punishable by imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both, while similar acts regarding land-marks fixed by the authority of a public servant are punishable by imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both. Use of fire or any explosive substance to damage property, including agricultural produce, is punishable by imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years and fine; however, using fire or explosive substance to destroy a building used as a place of worship, human dwelling, or place for the custody of property is punishable by imprisonment for life, or imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and fine.
BNS_S328:1407f5e7f5565b381c7fbe35b1251e1: Section 328 prescribes punishment for whoever intentionally runs any vessel aground or ashore, intending to commit theft of any property contained therein, to dishonestly misappropriate any such property, or with intent that such theft or misappropriation of property may be committed. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and the offender shall also be liable to fine. This section contains no Provisos, Exceptions, or Explanations.
BNS_S330:d6eb949850969eb9ecf2380a2a9168e: Section 330 defines "lurking house-trespass" as house-trespass committed with precautions to conceal the act from any person having a right to exclude or eject the trespasser from the building, tent, or vessel. "House-breaking" occurs when house-trespass involves entering or quitting a house in any of six ways: through a passage made by the person or an abettor; through a passage not intended for human entrance or by scaling/climbing; through a passage opened by means not intended by the occupier; by opening any lock; by using criminal force, assault, or threats of assault; or by unfastening a passage known to be fastened. The section includes an Explanation stating that any out-house or building occupied with a house, and between which there is immediate internal communication, is part of the house.
BNS_S113:ed44fc0be480d50c0a62f11a148d736: Section 113 defines a terrorist act as an act intended to threaten the unity, integrity, sovereignty, security, or economic security of India, strike terror, overawe public functionaries, or abduct persons to compel Governments using explosives, lethal weapons, or counterfeit Indian currency (as defined in the Explanations). If the act results in death, the punishment is death or imprisonment for life, whereas conspiracy, attempt, abetment, training, recruitment, or other terrorist acts are punishable with imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than five years but which may extend to imprisonment for life. Membership and knowing possession of terrorist-derived property are punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to imprisonment for life, while harbouring or concealing a person—subject to a Proviso exempting the spouse of the offender—is punishable with imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than three years but which may extend to imprisonment for life.
