BNS_S39:437a4fdc9aaad066cf72532812042adc: If the offence is not of any of the descriptions specified in section 38, the right of private defence of the body does not extend to the voluntary causing of death to the assailant. However, the right does extend, under the restrictions specified in section 37, to the voluntary causing to the assailant of any harm other than death.
BNS_S51:01e9729f92bb66393ee4ec23ef611224: Section 51 stipulates that when an act is abetted and a different act is done, the abettor is liable for the act done in the same manner and to the same extent as if he had directly abetted it. This liability is subject to a Proviso that the act done was a probable consequence of the abetment and was committed under the influence of the instigation, or with the aid or in pursuance of the conspiracy which constituted the abetment.
BNS_S19:11bd65847d6a1d154064d0062a7973b0: Section 19 stipulates that nothing is an offence merely by reason of an act being done with the knowledge that it is likely to cause harm, provided it is done without any criminal intention to cause harm and in good faith for the purpose of preventing or avoiding other harm to person or property. An Explanation clarifies that it is a question of fact whether the harm to be prevented or avoided was of such a nature and so imminent as to justify or excuse the risk of doing the act with the knowledge that it was likely to cause harm.
BNS_S47:292bcee6c3597510fc897459e4754e69: Section 47 stipulates that a person abets an offence within the meaning of this Sanhita who, in India, abets the commission of any act without and beyond India which would constitute an offence if committed in India. The section includes an illustration where a person in India instigates a foreigner in another country to commit a murder, rendering the individual guilty of abetting murder.
BNS_S66:30795e71c84953f4a26df3817b6d6e89: Section 66 stipulates that whoever commits an offence punishable under sub-section (1) or sub-section (2) of section 64 and inflicts an injury causing the death of the woman or causing the woman to be in a persistent vegetative state shall be punished. The penalty is rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than twenty years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person’s natural life, or with death.
BNS_S61:abde132b0e213b150e177012753c002e: Section 61 defines criminal conspiracy as an agreement between two or more persons with a common object to do, or cause to be done, an illegal act or an act which is not illegal by illegal means; a Proviso stipulates that for agreements other than to commit an offence, some act besides the agreement must be done in pursuance thereof, and an Explanation clarifies that the illegal act may be the ultimate or incidental object. A party to a criminal conspiracy to commit an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for a term of two years or upwards shall, where no express provision is made in this Sanhita, be punished in the same manner as if he had abetted such offence. Other criminal conspiracies shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term not exceeding six months, or with fine or with both.
BNS_S74:9d715eb99fb8dab0dc3a88b2a6feb5e9: Section 74 penalizes whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any woman, intending to outrage or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby outrage her modesty. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than one year but which may extend to five years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S80:366d6fcdba4e8720d4112e328e621502: Section 80 defines “dowry death” as the death of a woman caused by burns, bodily injury, or occurring otherwise than under normal circumstances within seven years of her marriage, provided she was subjected to cruelty or harassment by her husband or any relative of her husband soon before her death for, or in connection with, any demand for dowry. In such instances, the husband or relative shall be deemed to have caused her death, with an Explanation specifying that “dowry” shall have the same meaning as in section 2 of the Dowry Prohibition Act, 1961 (28 of 1961). Whoever commits dowry death shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than seven years but which may extend to imprisonment for life.
BNS_S37:a51633c839d31850d53ba3b90fcb87fd: Section 37 stipulates there is no right of private defence against acts or attempted acts that do not reasonably cause the apprehension of death or of grievous hurt if performed or directed by a public servant acting in good faith under colour of his office, even if not strictly justifiable by law, or where there is time to have recourse to the protection of the public authorities. The right of private defence in no case extends to the inflicting of more harm than it is necessary to inflict for the purpose of defence. Explanation 1 clarifies that the right is not deprived unless the actor is known or believed to be a public servant, and Explanation 2 states the right is not deprived against acts done by direction of a public servant unless the direction is known or the person fails to state authority or produce written authority if demanded.
BNS_S53:b4d5046e08881670c5e3e0533bc89976: If an act is abetted with the intention of causing a particular effect, but causes a different effect, the abettor is liable for that effect in the same manner and to the same extent as if they had abetted the act with the intention of causing that effect. This is subject to the proviso that the abettor knew that the act abetted was likely to cause that effect. As per the provided illustration, if an abettor instigates grievous hurt that is likely to cause death, they are liable to be punished with the punishment provided for murder.
BNS_S84:40855a573e4f0f1f805c6485050ac372: Section 84 criminalizes the act of taking, enticing away, concealing, or detaining a woman, whom the perpetrator knows or has reason to believe to be the wife of another man, with intent that she may have illicit intercourse with any person. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. This section contains no Provisos, Exceptions, or Explanations.
BNS_S3:899262bd457d194c6a658e9591162d843: All definitions, penal provisions, and illustrations are subject to the "General Exceptions" (such as a child under seven years of age), and expressions explained in any part of the Sanhita apply throughout. Property in the possession of a spouse, clerk, or servant—including those employed temporarily or on a particular occasion per the Explanation—is deemed in the person’s possession, and "acts" include "illegal omissions" unless contrary intention appears. Liability is assigned to persons acting in furtherance of a common intention, those possessing specific criminal knowledge or intention, or those causing an effect partly by an act and partly by an omission. Finally, individuals intentionally cooperating through several acts, singly or jointly, commit the offence, even if several persons engaged in a criminal act are guilty of different offences by means of that act.
BNS_S88:248118d992f1a4f69856f054bf906e73: Section 88 penalizes whoever voluntarily causes a woman with child to miscarry, unless such miscarriage is caused in good faith for the purpose of saving the life of the woman. The offence is punishable by imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both; however, if the woman be quick with child, the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine. An Explanation clarifies that a woman who causes herself to miscarry is within the meaning of this section.
BNS_S73:1c248eb20c469bfd32e1fc813d507e3c: Section 73 penalizes whoever prints or publishes any matter in relation to any proceeding before a Court with respect to an offence referred to in section 72 without the previous permission of such Court. Such an act shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years and shall also be liable to fine. An Explanation clarifies that the printing or publication of the judgment of any High Court or the Supreme Court does not amount to an offence within the meaning of this section.
BNS_S8:1b332b20afc4ff43c68f395a7e9add93f: Where no sum is expressed, the amount of fine is unlimited but shall not be excessive; for offences punishable with imprisonment and fine, imprisonment or fine, or fine only, the Court may direct imprisonment in default of fine in excess of any other imprisonment, which may be of any description allowed for the offence unless the offence is punishable with fine or community service, in which case such imprisonment shall be simple. For offences punishable with imprisonment as well as fine, default imprisonment shall not exceed one-fourth of the term of imprisonment which is the maximum fixed for the offence; if punishable with fine or community service, default imprisonment shall not exceed two months when the amount of the fine does not exceed five thousand rupees, four months when the amount of the fine does not exceed ten thousand rupees, or one year in any other case. Imprisonment in default of fine or community service terminates upon payment or levy of the fine, including proportional discharge as per the provided illustration, and unpaid fines may be levied within six years or the sentence duration if longer, with the offender's death not discharging liability for property legally liable for debts.
BNS_S90:cd61f0c5a026513e27653189fdf12f27: Section 90 penalizes death caused by act done with intent to cause miscarriage of a woman with child, which under sub-section (1) is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and fine. Sub-section (2) stipulates that if the act is done without the consent of the woman, it shall be punishable either with imprisonment for life, or with the punishment specified in said sub-section. An Explanation clarifies that it is not essential to this offence that the offender should know that the act is likely to cause death.
BNS_S89:72755bcbca8ca3463f4bc0014834c852: Section 89 prescribes punishment for causing miscarriage under section 88 without the consent of the woman, regardless of whether the woman is quick with child or not. Whoever commits this offence shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S92:f910401b97b48d48407047c671c66796: Section 92 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, defines the offence of causing death of a quick unborn child by an act amounting to culpable homicide, occurring when an act is performed under circumstances that would constitute culpable homicide if it caused death. The offender shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine. An illustration provided clarifies that if an act likely to cause the death of a pregnant woman results in the death of a quick unborn child without causing the death of the woman, the actor is guilty under this section.
BNS_S96:2a62b94c548456a7adf43215d3a28b35: Section 96 defines the offence of procuration of child, where whoever, by any means whatsoever, induces any child to go from any place or to do any act. This must be done with intent that such child may be, or knowing that it is likely that such child will be, forced or seduced to illicit intercourse with another person. The offender shall be punishable with imprisonment which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S78:313f4545e282351bd6be1c0b9fe305d1: Section 78 defines stalking as any man who follows a woman and contacts, or attempts to contact such woman to foster personal interaction repeatedly despite a clear indication of disinterest by such woman, or monitors the use by a woman of the internet, e-mail or any other form of electronic communication. A proviso stipulates such conduct shall not amount to stalking if the man proves it was pursued for the purpose of preventing or detecting crime while being entrusted with such responsibility by the State, pursued under any law or to comply with any condition or requirement imposed by any person under any law, or was reasonable and justified in the particular circumstances. On first conviction, the offender shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, and shall also be liable to fine; on a second or subsequent conviction, the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S86:075436c606332435b419b0aeb36f7ccd: For the purposes of section 85, “cruelty” means (a) any wilful conduct which is of such a nature as is likely to drive the woman to commit suicide or to cause grave injury or danger to life, limb or health (whether mental or physical) of the woman. It further includes (b) harassment of the woman where such harassment is with a view to coercing her or any person related to her to meet any unlawful demand for any property or valuable security or is on account of failure by her or any person related to her to meet such demand.
BNS_S104:d8a3df226e2b69ab763fdbc1f6440fd: Section 104 prescribes the punishment for murder by life-convict. Whoever, being under sentence of imprisonment for life, commits murder, shall be punished with death or with imprisonment for life. Such imprisonment for life shall mean the remainder of that person’s natural life.
BNS_S94:c1b432cf2b71475def079e96656d6dd4: Section 94 stipulates that whoever, by secretly burying or otherwise disposing of the dead body of a child, whether such child die before or after or during its birth, intentionally conceals or endeavours to conceal the birth of such child, commits the offence. The punishment for this act is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S100:1f212b131c8781e600b1d76402c1c12: Culpable homicide is committed by whoever causes death by doing an act with the intention of causing death, the intention of causing such bodily injury as is likely to cause death, or with the knowledge that he is likely by such act to cause death. Explanation 1 deems a person to have caused death if bodily injury accelerates the death of another labouring under a disorder, disease or bodily infirmity, while Explanation 2 stipulates that causing death by bodily injury is deemed causing death even if proper remedies and skilful treatment might have prevented it. Explanation 3 clarifies that causing the death of a child in the mother’s womb is not homicide, but may amount to culpable homicide if any part of a living child has been brought forth, regardless of whether the child breathed or was completely born.
BNS_S98:ea7cb07b25735f5e2e3f3922b7599d65: Whoever sells, lets to hire, or otherwise disposes of any child with intent that such child shall at any age be employed or used for prostitution, illicit intercourse, or any unlawful and immoral purpose, or knowing it to be likely, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years and shall also be liable to fine. Explanation 1 stipulates that when a female under the age of eighteen years is sold, let for hire, or otherwise disposed of to a prostitute or person managing a brothel, the disposer shall, until the contrary is proved, be presumed to have disposed of her with intent for prostitution. Explanation 2 defines “illicit intercourse” as sexual intercourse between persons not united by marriage or by any union or tie recognized by personal law or custom as constituting a quasi-marital relation.
BNS_S82:8fca62e9739fe85d993a5374699e0572: Whoever, having a husband or wife living, marries in any case in which such marriage is void by reason of its taking place during the life of such husband or wife, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine. An Exception applies to persons whose marriage has been declared void by a Court of competent jurisdiction, or those whose former husband or wife has been continually absent for the space of seven years and not heard of as being alive, provided the person informs the subsequent spouse of the real state of facts so far as the same are within his or her knowledge. Whoever commits the offence under sub-section (1) having concealed from the person with whom the subsequent marriage is contracted, the fact of the former marriage, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S114:8e44049229d411f366ec06ce8cb9d61: Section 114 defines the legal concept of "Hurt." It stipulates that whoever causes bodily pain, disease or infirmity to any person is said to cause hurt. This section contains no Provisos, Exceptions, or Explanations.
BNS_S68:79daaec70f4f199ef8e2781d81972472: Section 68 punishes any person in a position of authority or fiduciary relationship, a public servant, a superintendent or manager of a jail, remand home, or other place of custody established by or under any law for the time being in force, or a women’s or children’s institution, or someone on the management or staff of a hospital, who abuses such position or fiduciary relationship to induce or seduce any woman in their custody, charge, or present in the premises to have sexual intercourse not amounting to the offence of rape. The punishment is rigorous imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than five years, but which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine. Four Explanations are included: Explanation 1 and 2 define “sexual intercourse” via clauses (a) to (d) of section 63 and its Explanation 1; Explanation 3 includes any person holding office in such institutions who exercises authority or control over inmates as a “Superintendent”; and Explanation 4 defines “hospital” and “women’s or children’s institution” per clauses (b) and (d) of the Explanation to sub-section (2) of section 64.
BNS_S110:7f075497a65efd3664c25fb347c5135: Section 110 defines "Attempt to commit culpable homicide," applicable to whoever performs an act with such intention or knowledge and under such circumstances that, if death were caused, they would be guilty of "culpable homicide not amounting to murder." Such an act is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both. If hurt is caused to any person by such act, the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S62:3a70827fd38cde22eb62ea77bbccc842: Where no express provision is made by this Sanhita for the punishment of such attempt, whoever attempts to commit an offence punishable by this Sanhita with imprisonment for life or imprisonment, or to cause such an offence to be committed, by doing any act towards the commission of the offence, shall be punished with imprisonment of any description provided for the offence, for a term which may extend to one-half of the imprisonment for life or, as the case may be, one-half of the longest term of imprisonment provided for that offence, or with such fine as is provided for the offence, or with both. The section includes Illustrations where an act towards the commission, such as breaking open a box or thrusting a hand into a pocket, constitutes a guilty attempt even if the intended object is absent.
BNS_S116:e16ff37bbdaad4381128cfe30fd7dab: Section 116 designates only specific kinds of hurt as “grievous,” namely emasculation, permanent privation of the sight of either eye, permanent privation of the hearing of either ear, privation of any member or joint, and the destruction or permanent impairing of the powers of any member or joint. It further encompasses permanent disfiguration of the head or face, fracture or dislocation of a bone or tooth, and any hurt which endangers life. Additionally, hurt is classified as “grievous” if it causes the sufferer to be during the space of fifteen days in severe bodily pain or unable to follow his ordinary pursuits.
BNS_S118:f46a0d0e7f604fe6a4c3b4e1da84a32: Section 118(1) penalizes whoever, except as provided by sub-section (1) of section 122, voluntarily causes hurt via an instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, a weapon of offence likely to cause death, fire, heated substance, poison, corrosive substance, explosive substance, any substance deleterious to inhale, swallow, or receive into the blood, or any animal, punishable by imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or fine which may extend to twenty thousand rupees, or both. Section 118(2) stipulates that whoever, except as provided by sub-section (2) of section 122, voluntarily causes grievous hurt by the means referred to in sub-section (1) shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than one year but which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S112:643e5a19839576fda50adb9507d9c69: Section 112 defines petty organised crime as any act of theft, snatching, cheating, unauthorised selling of tickets, unauthorised betting or gambling, selling of public examination question papers, or any other similar criminal act committed by a member of a group or gang, either singly or jointly. An Explanation provides that “theft” includes trick theft, theft from vehicle, dwelling house or business premises, cargo theft, pick pocketing, theft through card skimming, shoplifting and theft of Automated Teller Machine. Whoever commits any petty organised crime shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than one year but which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S122:2c14ab96d34fa3b6c7f5a4b17803f0d: Section 122 (1) mandates that whoever voluntarily causes hurt on grave and sudden provocation, provided they neither intend nor know themselves to be likely to cause hurt to any person other than the person who gave the provocation, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both. Section 122 (2) stipulates that whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt on grave and sudden provocation, provided they neither intend nor know themselves to be likely to cause grievous hurt to any person other than the person who gave the provocation, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both. An Explanation specifies that this section is subject to the same proviso as Exception 1 of section 101.
BNS_S109:8b9fcc54022239a0165e38f9097fc04: Section 109(1) provides that whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge, and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine; if hurt is caused to any person by such act, the offender shall be liable either to imprisonment for life, or to such punishment as is hereinbefore mentioned. Sub-section (2) stipulates that when any person offending under sub-section (1) is under sentence of imprisonment for life and hurt is caused, he may be punished with death or with imprisonment for life, which shall mean the remainder of that person’s natural life.
BNS_S60:725a2db505d6b829712268ea0c151f41: Section 60 applies to whoever, intending to facilitate or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby facilitate the commission of an offence punishable with imprisonment, voluntarily conceals, by any act or illegal omission, the existence of a design to commit such offence, or makes any representation which he knows to be false respecting such design. If the offence be committed, the person shall be punished with imprisonment of the description provided for the offence, for a term which may extend to one-fourth. If the offence be not committed, the person shall be punished to one-eighth, of the longest term of such imprisonment, or with such fine as is provided for the offence, or with both.
BNS_S119:ebcfb0b5dea4bc410b68bdf61c3d838: Under Section 119(1), whoever voluntarily causes hurt for the purpose of extorting any property or valuable security from the sufferer or any person interested in the sufferer, or of constraining the sufferer or any person interested in such sufferer to do anything which is illegal or which may facilitate the commission of an offence, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine. Under Section 119(2), whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt for any purpose referred to in sub-section (1) shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S123:7deb6bf0ae4a865ea41fc3fba66c4c9: Section 123 applies to any person who administers to or causes to be taken by any person any poison or any stupefying, intoxicating or unwholesome drug, or other thing, with intent to cause hurt, intent to commit or to facilitate the commission of an offence, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause hurt. Such an act shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and the offender shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S106:9f3cc667ca7418fe5328ffc22576781: Subsection (1) provides that whoever causes death of any person by doing any rash or negligent act not amounting to culpable homicide shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, and shall also be liable to fine; if such act is done by a registered medical practitioner while performing medical procedure, he shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, and shall also be liable to fine. An Explanation defines “registered medical practitioner” as a medical practitioner who possesses any medical qualification recognised under the National Medical Commission Act, 2019 (30 of 2019) and whose name has been entered in the National Medical Register or a State Medical Register under that Act. Subsection (2) states that whoever causes death of any person by rash and negligent driving of vehicle not amounting to culpable homicide, and escapes without reporting it to a police officer or a Magistrate soon after the incident, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description of a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S124:f3bf84d8501fe5748579077bd770567: Section 124(1) penalizes whoever causes permanent or partial damage or deformity, burns, maiming, disfigurement, disability, grievous hurt, or a permanent vegetative state by throwing/administering acid or using other means with intention or knowledge, punishable by imprisonment for a term not less than ten years but which may extend to imprisonment for life, and with fine; two Provisos mandate the fine be just and reasonable to meet the victim's medical expenses and be paid to the victim. Section 124(2) prescribes imprisonment for a term not less than five years but which may extend to seven years, and fine, for attempting to throw or administer acid or use other means with the intention of causing such injuries. Explanation 1 defines “acid” as any substance of acidic, corrosive, or burning nature capable of causing bodily injury leading to scars, disfigurement, or temporary or permanent disability, while Explanation 2 clarifies that permanent or partial damage or deformity or permanent vegetative state shall not be required to be irreversible.
BNS_S126:4901820662b792a1216d32e2947fdf1: Section 126 defines wrongful restraint as whoever voluntarily obstructs any person so as to prevent that person from proceeding in any direction in which that person has a right to proceed. This section includes an Exception for the obstruction of a private way over land or water which a person in good faith believes himself to have a lawful right to obstruct. Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S132:ae8ffe6160e941732cf315cbfa4b577: Section 132 penalizes whoever assaults or uses criminal force against a public servant in the execution of his duty, with intent to prevent or deter the discharge of his duty, or in consequence of anything done or attempted to be done in the lawful discharge of his duty. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S133:0a94b459a404cadf63bf46ba347771b: Section 133 penalizes whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, intending thereby to dishonour that person, otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S134:4def239c790ca75bcae2abc04f17d6e: Section 134 penalizes whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person in attempting to commit theft on any property which that person is then wearing or carrying. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S120:33994605b66ca27605b75bdbda517a0: Section 120(1) penalizes whoever voluntarily causes hurt to extort confession or information leading to the detection of an offence or misconduct from the sufferer or any person interested in the sufferer, or to constrain them to restore or cause the restoration of any property or valuable security, satisfy any claim or demand, or give information leading to such restoration, with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and a fine. Section 120(2) stipulates that whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt for any purpose referred to in sub-section (1) shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine. This section contains Illustrations.
BNS_S128:75780c1fe3be0c09f3ebce029ab0765: A person is said to use force to another if he causes motion, change of motion, or cessation of motion to that other, or causes to any substance such motion, change of motion, or cessation of motion as brings that substance into contact with any part of that other’s body, anything the other is wearing or carrying, or anything so situated that such contact affects that other’s sense of feeling. The section includes a Proviso stating that the motion, change of motion, or cessation of motion must be caused in one of three ways: (a) by his own bodily power; (b) by disposing any substance in such a manner that the motion, change of motion, or cessation of motion takes place without any further act on his part, or on the part of any other person; or (c) by inducing any animal to move, to change its motion, or to cease to move.
BNS_S131:7ddaa44c031447b59720a7d87a6774b: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both. Explanation 1 stipulates that grave and sudden provocation will not mitigate punishment if the provocation is sought or voluntarily provoked by the offender as an excuse, given by anything done in obedience to the law or by a public servant in the lawful exercise of powers, or given by anything done in the lawful exercise of the right of private defence. Explanation 2 further clarifies that whether the provocation was grave and sudden enough to mitigate the offence is a question of fact.
BNS_S136:f795e711b9605eb2dbecc6a3617bee2: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person on grave and sudden provocation given by that person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both. An Explanation states that this section is subject to the same Explanation as section 131 of kidnapping, abduction, slavery and forced labour.
BNS_S121:4477a37d0916c5f9bbc1c52e82b7fbb: Section 121(1) punishes whoever voluntarily causes hurt to any person being a public servant in the discharge of his duty as such public servant, or with intent to prevent or deter that person or any other public servant from discharging his duty as such public servant, or in consequence of anything done or attempted to be done by that person in the lawful discharge of his duty as such public servant, with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, or with fine, or with both. Under subsection (2), whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt to any person being a public servant in the discharge of his duty as such public servant, or with intent to prevent or deter that person or any other public servant from discharging his duty as such public servant, or in consequence of anything done or attempted to be done by that person in the lawful discharge of his duty as such public servant, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than one year but which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S140:e4c8a07010aa9867fca5166fe997683: Section 140 penalizes kidnapping or abducting a person to be murdered or put in danger of being murdered with imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and fine. Kidnapping, abducting, or detaining a person—through threats, reasonable apprehension, or causing hurt or death—to compel the Government, any foreign State, international inter-governmental organisation, or any other person to do or abstain from any act or pay ransom is punishable with death, or imprisonment for life, and fine. Furthermore, kidnapping or abducting for secret and wrongful confinement is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years and fine, while kidnapping or abducting for the purpose of, or danger of, grievous hurt, slavery, or the unnatural lust of any person is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years and fine.
BNS_S146:079fd2c4e0c8ec5c2fcfb81eae45023: Section 146 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, defines the offence of Unlawful compulsory labour, committed by whoever unlawfully compels any person to labour against the will of that person. The prescribed punishment for this offence is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S102:a0829af2be00edf4832ffae80d4536d: If a person, by doing anything which he intends or knows to be likely to cause death, commits culpable homicide by causing the death of any person whose death he neither intends nor knows himself to be likely to cause, the offense is categorized accordingly. Specifically, the culpable homicide committed by the offender is of the description of which it would have been if he had caused the death of the person whose death he intended or knew himself to be likely to cause.
BNS_S149:cbf92dec73b4de122caf4d4652f0ccf: Section 149 stipulates that whoever collects men, arms or ammunition or otherwise prepares to wage war with the intention of either waging or being prepared to wage war against the Government of India commits an offence. This act is punishable with imprisonment for life or imprisonment of either description for a term not exceeding ten years, and the offender shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S141:7dc5a47eb650f2613cf535d8791c35b: Section 141 applies to whoever imports into India from any country outside India any girl under the age of twenty-one years or any boy under the age of eighteen years with intent that the girl or boy may be, or knowing it to be likely that the girl or boy will be, forced or seduced to illicit intercourse with another person. Such persons shall be punishable with imprisonment which may extend to ten years and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S152:a6c47697189c24e9699b2a0707c8d84: Section 152 penalizes whoever purposely or knowingly, by words (spoken or written), signs, visible representation, electronic communication, use of financial mean, or otherwise, excites or attempts to excite secession, armed rebellion, or subversive activities, encourages feelings of separatist activities, or endangers sovereignty or unity and integrity of India. Whoever indulges in or commits any such act shall be punished with imprisonment for life or with imprisonment which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine. An Explanation clarifies that comments expressing disapprobation of Government measures, or administrative or other action, to obtain their alteration by lawful means without exciting the referred activities do not constitute an offence.
BNS_S148:d45ba57e37e858a80c6f821b3c61848: Whoever, within or without and beyond India, conspires to commit any of the offences punishable by section 147, or conspires to overawe the Central Government or any State Government by means of criminal force or the show of criminal force, shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine. An Explanation stipulates that to constitute a conspiracy under this section, it is not necessary that any act or illegal omission shall take place in pursuance thereof.
BNS_S154:ffc5c421cb50d132fe14bee924779a3: Section 154 penalizes whoever commits depredation, or makes preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of any foreign State at peace with the Government of India. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and the offender shall also be liable to fine. Additionally, the section mandates the forfeiture of any property used or intended to be used in committing such depredation, or acquired by such depredation.
BNS_S153:61d2ce036e805a9ad348ae988cc9774: Section 153 penalizes whoever wages war against the Government of any foreign State at peace with the Government of India, attempts to wage such war, or abets the waging of such war. Punishment includes imprisonment for life, to which fine may be added, or imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, to which fine may be added, or fine.
BNS_S160:01c2075680f124e1fe1dee9e9db0afe: Section 160 penalizes whoever abets the committing of mutiny by an officer, soldier, sailor or airman in the Army, Navy or Air Force of the Government of India. If mutiny is committed in consequence of that abetment, the offender shall be punished with death, imprisonment for life, or imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S150:ff0d87aac4c759eb608b7144e329a56: Section 150 penalizes whoever, by any act or by any illegal omission, conceals the existence of a design to wage war against the Government of India. The offence involves intending by such concealment to facilitate, or knowing it to be likely that such concealment will facilitate, the waging of such war. The punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and the person shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S159:c7a43013a44091995130eb32fde3bd8: Section 159 penalizes whoever abets the committing of mutiny by an officer, soldier, sailor or airman, in the Army, Navy or Air Force of the Government of India, or attempts to seduce any such officer, soldier, sailor or airman from his allegiance or his duty. Such an individual shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S156:1f8bea2d51e228fb7e2ba41e995a7fd: Under Section 156, a public servant having the custody of any State prisoner or prisoner of war who voluntarily allows such prisoner to escape from any place in which such prisoner is confined is liable for punishment. The offender shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S158:11c92bca96f0b2b059467b4e8fb0e9d: Section 158 penalizes whoever knowingly aids or assists any State prisoner or prisoner of war in escaping from lawful custody, rescues or attempts to rescue such prisoner, harbours or conceals such prisoner who has escaped from lawful custody, or offers or attempts to offer any resistance to the recapture of such prisoner. The punishment is imprisonment for life, or imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine. The Explanation stipulates that a State prisoner or prisoner of war permitted to be at large on his parole within certain limits in India is said to escape from lawful custody if he goes beyond those limits.
BNS_S168:eed52d29f2567d802003afbf395d511: Section 168 penalizes any person, not being a soldier, sailor or airman in the Army, Naval or Air service of the Government of India, who wears any garb or carries any token resembling any garb or token used by such soldier, sailor or airman. The act must be committed with the intention that it may be believed that he is such a soldier, sailor or airman. Punishment consists of imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to two thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S166:e1042321b8ff1c76e75f6e274c6aece: Whoever abets what he knows to be an act of insubordination by an officer, soldier, sailor or airman, in the Army, Navy or Air Force, of the Government of India, shall be punished if such act of insubordination is committed in consequence of that abetment. The prescribed punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S139:271f0554bdd0f524e1aad0142dafdd7: Whoever kidnaps a child or, not being the lawful guardian, obtains custody for employment or use for the purposes of begging is punishable with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than ten years but which may extend to imprisonment for life and a fine, while maiming a child for such purposes is punishable with imprisonment which shall not be less than twenty years, but which may extend to life which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person’s natural life, and a fine. It shall be presumed, unless the contrary is proved, that any person, not being the lawful guardian, who employs or uses a child for the purposes of begging, kidnapped or otherwise obtained the custody of such child for that purpose. The term “begging” is defined as soliciting or receiving alms in a public place (under the pretence of singing, dancing, fortune telling, performing tricks, or selling articles), entering private premises for soliciting alms, exposing or exhibiting a sore, wound, injury, deformity, or disease (of self, another person, or an animal) to obtain or extort alms, or using such child as an exhibit for such purposes.
BNS_S176:28d767ef4c8ab2c54076582fae63480: Section 176 penalizes whoever, without general or special authority in writing of a candidate, incurs or authorises expenses for public meetings, advertisements, circulars, publications, or any other way to promote or procure the election of such candidate, with a fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees. A Proviso states that if a person incurs expenses not exceeding the amount of ten rupees without authority but obtains approval in writing of the candidate within ten days from the date such expenses were incurred, they shall be deemed to have incurred such expenses with the authority of the candidate.
BNS_S174:c13b772bb96b0fc1b1a5828fb96ebbd: Section 174 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, mandates punishment for whoever commits the offence of undue influence or personation at an election. Such an individual shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S180:f8e62eee47ae8e51eb5c8e9e50eaa0e: Section 180 penalizes whoever possesses any forged or counterfeit coin, stamp, currency-note or bank-note, knowing or having reason to believe the same to be forged or counterfeit and intending to use the same as genuine or that it may be used as genuine. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both. An Explanation specifies that possession established to be from a lawful source shall not constitute an offence under this section.
BNS_S162:52b9f487bb6d1a800f77a97a510cdcf: Whoever abets an assault by an officer, soldier, sailor or airman, in the Army, Navy or Air Force of the Government of India, on any superior officer being in the execution of his office, shall be punished if such assault is committed in consequence of that abetment. The punishment consists of imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and the person shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S188:aae80ca9872a03145b6df6c850cf03a: Section 188 penalizes whoever, without lawful authority, takes any coining tool or instrument out of any mint lawfully established in India. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and the perpetrator shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S143:0286988f3b264fdac369212285219fa: Section 143 defines trafficking as recruiting, transporting, harbouring, transferring, or receiving a person or persons for exploitation—which per Explanation 1 includes any act of physical exploitation or any form of sexual exploitation, slavery or practices similar to slavery, servitude, beggary or forced removal of organs—via threats, force, coercion, abduction, fraud, deception, abuse of power, or inducement (including the giving or receiving of payments or benefits), noting per Explanation 2 that victim consent is immaterial. Penalties include rigorous imprisonment for a term not less than seven years, but which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine for one person; not less than ten years but which may extend to imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine for more than one person; not less than ten years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine for a child; and not less than fourteen years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine for more than one child. If a person is convicted of the offence of trafficking of a child on more than one occasion, or if a public servant or police officer is involved, they shall be punished with imprisonment for life, which shall mean imprisonment for the remainder of that person’s natural life, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S184:acf698de76ba4d3daa28c1e02f39de7: Section 184 penalizes whoever, fraudulently or with intent to cause loss to the Government, uses for any purpose a stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue which he knows to have been before used. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S194:7f71641b796bbe0a55d7a07d14edbcd: Section 194 defines "affray" as occurring when two or more persons, by fighting in a public place, disturb the public peace. Whoever commits an affray shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S192:dcd2bf5bd5b92968036b4149c7c16e1: Section 192 penalizes whoever malignantly or wantonly does anything illegal to give provocation to any person, intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause the offence of rioting to be committed. If the offence of rioting is committed in consequence of such provocation, the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both. If the offence of rioting be not committed, the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S199:e96056764b9bca5fda90cf1a2408d1e: Section 199 penalizes any public servant who knowingly disobeys a direction of law prohibiting the required attendance of any person for investigation into an offence or other matter, or knowingly disobeys any direction regulating the manner of such investigation to the prejudice of any person. It further criminalizes the failure to record information given under sub-section (1) of section 173 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023 concerning cognizable offences punishable under sections 64, 65, 66, 67, 68, 70, 71, 74, 76, 77, 79, 124, 143, or 144. The punishment is rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than six months but which may extend to two years, and shall also be liable to fine.
BNS_S195:1048d09d0f2ca2b3335ea2b46dfa9fa: Under subsection (1), whoever assaults, obstructs, or uses criminal force on any public servant in the discharge of his duty in endeavouring to disperse an unlawful assembly, or to suppress a riot or affray, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine which shall not be less than twenty-five thousand rupees, or with both. Under subsection (2), whoever threatens to assault or attempts to obstruct, or threatens or attempts to use criminal force to any public servant in the discharge of his duty in endeavouring to disperse an unlawful assembly, or to suppress a riot or affray, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S185:d9b9c373e9b5c2a36087d9ff2896bb7: Whoever, fraudulently or with intent to cause loss to Government, erases or removes from a stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue any mark, put or impressed upon such stamp for the purpose of denoting that the same has been used, is liable for punishment. This offence also applies to anyone who knowingly has in his possession, or sells or disposes of, any such stamp from which such mark has been erased or removed, or sells or disposes of any such stamp which he knows to have been used. Such persons shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S210:4bbc68d3f2416b357cb789a1299d6a6: Section 210 penalizes whoever, being legally bound to produce or deliver up any document or electronic record to any public servant, intentionally omits to produce or deliver up the same. For a general public servant, the punishment is simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both. Where the document or electronic record is to be produced or delivered up to a Court, the punishment is simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both. The section includes an Illustration concerning the intentional omission to produce a document before a District Court.
BNS_S204:b975d02ec55fe69d522e36ba81d66d2: Section 204 addresses personating a public servant, which occurs when a person pretends to hold any particular office as a public servant, knowing that he does not hold such office, or falsely personates any other person holding such office. The offence is committed when the individual, in such assumed character, does or attempts to do any act under colour of such office. The perpetrator shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than six months but which may extend to three years and with fine.
BNS_S218:5606b96af21be274b87f45c0f362909: Section 218 penalizes any person who offers any resistance to the taking of any property by the lawful authority of any public servant, knowing or having reason to believe that he is such a public servant. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S201:9b88b8cd61cf5ba4b0720181809e681: A public servant charged with the preparation or translation of any document or electronic record commits an offence if they frame, prepare, or translate that document or electronic record in a manner which he knows or believes to be incorrect. Such an act must be committed intending thereby to cause, or knowing it to be likely to cause, injury to any person. The punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S227:528081748e8dbe89d9064879d35904a: Section 227 defines "Giving false evidence" as the act of any person, legally bound by an oath or express provision of law to state the truth, or bound by law to make a declaration, making a statement they know or believe to be false or do not believe to be true. Explanation 1 clarifies that a statement falls within this section whether made verbally or otherwise. Explanation 2 specifies that false statements regarding the belief of the person attesting are included, such as stating they believe a thing they do not believe or know a thing they do not know.
BNS_S221:c372659989a2f922541766c73d253f5: Section 221 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, stipulates that whoever voluntarily obstructs any public servant in the discharge of his public functions shall be punished. The prescribed penalty is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to two thousand and five hundred rupees, or with both.
BNS_S193:a81e190699bff1fd3c7b72e2479db2f: Under Section 193(1), the owner, occupier, or any person having or claiming an interest in land upon which an unlawful assembly or riot takes place is punishable with fine not exceeding one thousand rupees if they or their agent or manager, knowing or having reason to believe the offence is being or has been committed, or is likely to be committed, fail to give the earliest notice to the officer in charge at the nearest police station and fail to use all lawful means in their power to prevent, disperse, or suppress it. Subsections (2) and (3) provide that if a riot is committed for the benefit or on behalf of an owner, occupier, person claiming interest in the land or dispute subject, or a person who has accepted or derived benefit, such person or their agent or manager is punishable with fine if, having reason to believe the riot or unlawful assembly was likely, they fail to use all lawful means in their power to prevent and for suppressing and dispersing the same.
BNS_S222:bdb84638d34ed80b819840e926776a2: Section 222 penalizes whoever, being bound by law to render or furnish assistance to any public servant in the execution of his public duty, intentionally omits to give such assistance. Such omission is punishable with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to two thousand and five hundred rupees, or with both. Where assistance is demanded by a public servant legally competent to make such demand for executing any process lawfully issued by a Court, preventing the commission of an offence, suppressing a riot or affray, or apprehending a person charged with or guilty of an offence or having escaped from lawful custody, the punishment is simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S178:718e89a3790716e871a25d04c87c0d5: Whoever counterfeits, or knowingly performs any part of the process of counterfeiting, any coin, stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue, currency-note or bank-note, shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine. The section provides five Explanations: "bank-note" means a promissory note or engagement for the payment of money to bearer on demand issued by banking businesses or State/Sovereign Powers intended as a substitute for money, and "coin" carries the meaning assigned in section 2 of the Coinage Act, 2011, including metal stamped and issued by a State or Sovereign Power. These Explanations further define "counterfeiting Government stamp" as causing a genuine stamp of one denomination to appear like a genuine stamp of a different denomination, and "counterfeiting coin" as causing a genuine coin to appear like a different coin through intended or likely deception, or by diminishing the weight, altering the composition, or altering the appearance of the coin.
BNS_S170:c343c3a5a8654b68c6caa21165dccc3: Section 170 defines bribery as giving a gratification to induce or reward the exercise of any electoral right, or accepting gratification for oneself or another for exercising or inducing such rights; a proviso specifies that a declaration of public policy or a promise of public action shall not be an offence. Deeming provisions establish that offering, agreeing to give, or attempting to procure a gratification is deemed to give a gratification, and obtaining, agreeing to accept, or attempting to obtain a gratification is deemed to accept a gratification. Finally, a person who accepts a gratification as a motive for doing what he does not intend to do, or as a reward for doing what he has not done, shall be deemed to have accepted the gratification as a reward.
BNS_S190:7b0e3924617dc8e15f08d39b7fb66e5: Every person who, at the time of the committing of an offence, is a member of an unlawful assembly is guilty of that offence if the offence is committed by any member in prosecution of the common object of that assembly. This liability also extends to offences such as the members of that assembly knew to be likely to be committed in prosecution of that object.
BNS_S182:5aee9efee3cddaf60a86505e94eb2e8: Whoever makes, causes to be made, uses for any purpose whatsoever, or delivers to any person any document purporting to be, or in any way resembling, or so nearly resembling as to be calculated to deceive, any currency-note or bank-note shall be punished with fine which may extend to three hundred rupees. If any person whose name appears on a document the making of which is an offence under sub-section (1) refuses, without lawful excuse, to disclose to a police officer on being so required the name and address of the person by whom it was printed or otherwise made, he shall be punished with fine which may extend to six hundred rupees. Additionally, where a person's name appears on a document charged under sub-section (1) or on any other document used or distributed in connection with that document, it may, until the contrary is proved, be presumed that the person caused the document to be made.
BNS_S236:c18cc3c49bb3b9d04a6ba994206b3f1: Whoever makes a statement in a declaration made or subscribed by him, which any Court, public servant, or other person is bound or authorised by law to receive as evidence of any fact, commits an offence if that statement is false and the person knows or believes it to be false or does not believe it to be true. The statement must touch any point material to the object for which the declaration is made or used. Such a person shall be punished in the same manner as if he gave false evidence.
BNS_S239:34f67c7f645b4f93961c95fc4c199cd: Section 239 penalizes the "Intentional omission to give information of offence by person bound to inform" where whoever, knowing or having reason to believe that an offence has been committed, intentionally omits to give information respecting that offence which he is legally bound to give. The punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both. This section contains no Provisos, Exceptions, or Explanations.
BNS_S243:aeb1823eb04d6c504ce354d33328a5b: Section 243 penalizes whoever fraudulently removes, conceals, transfers, or delivers any property or interest therein to prevent it from being taken as a forfeiture or in satisfaction of a fine under a sentence pronounced, or known to be likely to be pronounced, by a Court or other competent authority. It similarly applies to preventing property from being taken in execution of a decree or order made, or known to be likely to be made, by a Court in a civil suit. The punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S247:ee8ac50246548af2a4fe51b7fe7675e: Section 247 penalizes whoever fraudulently obtains a decree or order against any person for a sum not due, for a larger sum than is due, or for any property or interest in property to which he is not entitled. It further covers whoever fraudulently causes a decree or order to be executed after it has been satisfied, or for anything in respect of which it has been satisfied, or fraudulently suffers or permits any such act to be done in his name. The punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S229:4c0594967533ce666e4265ae88b315d: Whoever intentionally gives false evidence in any stage of a judicial proceeding, or fabricates false evidence for the purpose of being used in any stage of a judicial proceeding, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees. For any case other than that referred to in sub-section (1), whoever intentionally gives or fabricates false evidence shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, and shall also be liable to fine which may extend to five thousand rupees. Explanations 1, 2, and 3 clarify that a trial before a Court-martial is a judicial proceeding, and that an investigation directed by law preliminary to a proceeding before a Court, or an investigation directed by a Court according to law and conducted under the authority of a Court, is a stage of a judicial proceeding.
BNS_S241:e26d2e01d1e4a43c82e2236f10e4214: Whoever secretes, destroys, obliterates, or renders illegible the whole or any part of any document or electronic record which he may be lawfully compelled to produce as evidence in a Court or in any proceeding lawfully held before a public servant, as such, commits an offence. This applies when done with the intention of preventing the document or electronic record from being produced or used as evidence, or after he shall have been lawfully summoned or required to produce the same for that purpose. The punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S238:6fee6c0afa38f0b898b43f00f11a9b1: Section 238 penalizes whoever, knowing or having reason to believe that an offence has been committed, causes any evidence of the commission of that offence to disappear, or gives any information respecting the offence which he knows or believes to be false, with the intention of screening the offender from legal punishment. If the offence is punishable with death, the offender shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years and shall also be liable to fine; if the offence is punishable with imprisonment for life or imprisonment which may extend to ten years, the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years and shall also be liable to fine. For offences punishable with imprisonment for any term not extending to ten years, the punishment is imprisonment of the description provided for the offence for a term which may extend to one-fourth part of the longest term of the imprisonment provided for the offence, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S255:aba4b43a006eb07b6f890001d2ba50b: Section 255 penalizes any public servant who knowingly disobeys any direction of the law regarding the way in which he is to conduct himself as such public servant. This applies if the act is intended to save, or known to be likely to save, any person from legal punishment, subject them to a less punishment than to which he is liable, or save any property from forfeiture or any charge to which it is liable by law. The offense is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S251:b19ab109c2a86d87baa1cc7f717da44: Section 251 penalizes whoever gives, causes, offers, or agrees to give any gratification, or restores or causes the restoration of any property, in consideration of any person concealing an offence, screening any person from legal punishment, or not proceeding against any person to bring them to legal punishment. Punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years and fine if the offence is punishable with death, or imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years and fine if the offence is punishable with imprisonment for life or with imprisonment which may extend to ten years. If the offence is punishable with imprisonment not extending to ten years, the penalty is imprisonment of the description provided for the offence for a term which may extend to one-fourth part of the longest term of imprisonment provided for the offence, or with fine, or with both. An Exception provides that the provisions of this section and section 250 do not extend to any case in which the offence may lawfully be compounded.
BNS_S258:ce556f2d0a7272ab08a9f2e3082ab5e: Section 258 penalizes any person holding an office with legal authority to commit persons for trial or confinement, or to keep persons in confinement, who corruptly or maliciously exercises such authority. The offence is committed when the individual acts knowing that in so doing they are acting contrary to law. The prescribed punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S223:a18d67d132c7b4a93d9a00d51f35c5f: Section 223 penalizes anyone who, knowing an order was promulgated by a public servant lawfully empowered to do so, disobeys a direction to abstain from an act or to take certain order with property in their possession or under management. Under clause (a), disobedience causing or tending to cause obstruction, annoyance or injury, or risk thereof, to any person lawfully employed, is punishable with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or a fine which may extend to two thousand and five hundred rupees, or both; under clause (b), disobedience causing or tending to cause danger to human life, health or safety, or a riot or affray, is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or a fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or both. The Explanation clarifies that the offender need not intend to produce harm or contemplate its likelihood, provided they know of the order and the disobedience produces, or is likely to produce, harm.
BNS_S267:4c427cf764f6293127e4eb18b994812: Section 267 penalizes whoever intentionally offers any insult or causes any interruption to any public servant while such public servant is sitting in any stage of a judicial proceeding. The punishment is simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S264:3da8a0f3ecfd6cdf6fcdf846a7883b7: Section 264 penalizes any public servant legally bound to apprehend or keep in confinement any person—in cases not provided for in section 259, section 260, section 261, or any other law for the time being in force—who omits to apprehend that person or suffers him to escape. If committed intentionally, the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both. If committed negligently, the punishment is simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S261:7b2b702cc16065d060ff6ca04877c7c: A public servant legally bound as such public servant to keep in confinement any person charged with or convicted of any offence or lawfully committed to custody commits an offence if they negligently suffer such person to escape from confinement. The punishment for this offence is simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S262:d5196a481cddb81b534a82d59465d58: Section 262 penalizes whoever intentionally offers any resistance or illegal obstruction to the lawful apprehension of himself for any offence with which he is charged or of which he has been convicted, or escapes or attempts to escape from any custody in which he is lawfully detained for any such offence. The punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. An Explanation specifies that this punishment is in addition to the punishment for which the person to be apprehended or detained in custody was liable for the offence with which he was charged or convicted.
BNS_S273:310a066ce24e2af8c9b530f4815e950: Section 273 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, stipulates that whoever knowingly disobeys any rule made by the Government for putting any mode of transport into a state of quarantine, for regulating the intercourse of any such transport in a state of quarantine, or for regulating the intercourse between places where an infectious disease prevails and other places, shall be punished. The penalty for such disobedience is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S245:4a971d0c5bf0e416d35193958b936df: Section 245 penalizes whoever fraudulently causes or suffers a decree or order to be passed at the suit of any person for a sum not due, a larger sum than is due, or for any property or interest in property to which such person is not entitled, or fraudulently causes or suffers a decree or order to be executed against them after it has been satisfied, or for anything in respect of which it has been satisfied. Such persons shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. An illustration clarifies that fraudulently suffering a judgment for a larger amount to enable another to share in sale proceeds of property under a decree constitutes this offence.
BNS_S279:525d8d8c3b12a7cc4d12a9e62cce356: Section 279 criminalizes the act of whoever voluntarily corrupts or fouls the water of any public spring or reservoir so as to render it less fit for the purpose for which it is ordinarily used. Such offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S276:e995ef528bf5ca1f1922aed52ada652: Section 276 penalizes whoever adulterates any drug or medical preparation in a manner to lessen the efficacy, change the operation, or make it noxious. This applies when the actor intends or knows it is likely that the substance will be sold or used for any medicinal purpose as if it had not undergone such adulteration. The punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S265:e88e9091f65b279d35e837a6824c4ed: In any case not provided for in section 262, section 263, or any other law for the time being in force, Section 265 applies to whoever intentionally offers any resistance or illegal obstruction to the lawful apprehension of himself or any other person. This section further covers anyone who escapes or attempts to escape from any custody in which he is lawfully detained, or rescues or attempts to rescue any other person from any custody in which that person is lawfully detained. Such acts shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S257:8839c76ef25f56a096e83c44839ad7e: A public servant who, corruptly or maliciously, makes or pronounces in any stage of a judicial proceeding any report, order, verdict, or decision which he knows to be contrary to law is liable to punishment. The prescribed punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
BNS_S259:ad9f91e632bac4961f201d640f4c088: A public servant legally bound to apprehend or keep in confinement any person charged with or liable to be apprehended for an offence commits an offence if they intentionally omit to apprehend such person, intentionally suffer such person to escape, or intentionally aid such person in escaping or attempting to escape from such confinement. Punishment is (a) imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, with or without fine, if the person was charged with, or liable to be apprehended for, an offence punishable with death; (b) imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, with or without fine, if the person was charged with, or liable to be apprehended for, an offence punishable with imprisonment for life or imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years; or (c) imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, with or without fine, if the person was charged with, or liable to be apprehended for, an offence punishable with imprisonment for a term less than ten years.
BNS_S282:b16732335219d70fd823d1e72221fd2: Section 282 penalizes whoever navigates any vessel in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person. The offence is punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S281:442c700e3bccd1ade58de0eb6b60ca6: Section 281 penalizes whoever drives any vehicle, or rides, on any public way in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person. The punishment for this offence is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S277:507f9145cb406d21af581139b457eec: Whoever, knowing any drug or medical preparation to have been adulterated to lessen its efficacy, change its operation, or render it noxious, sells, offers or exposes it for sale, issues it from any dispensary for medicinal purposes as unadulterated, or causes it to be used for medicinal purposes by any person not knowing of the adulteration, commits an offence under Section 277. Such person shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S275:7768916137286d499a5af676496eddb: Section 275 penalizes whoever sells, or offers or exposes for sale, as food or drink, any article which has been rendered or has become noxious, or is in a state unfit for food or drink, knowing or having reason to believe that the same is noxious as food or drink. Such person shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
BNS_S283:8cd51f81e6a324996e1757846eb542d: Whoever exhibits any false light, mark or buoy, intending or knowing it to be likely that such exhibition will mislead any navigator, shall be punished. The punishment consists of imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and with fine which shall not be less than ten thousand rupees.
BNS_S249:aca51f070bea5c88f024c26e4bfeaa1: Section 249 penalizes whoever harbours or conceals a person whom he knows or has reason to believe to be the offender, with the intention of screening him from legal punishment. The offender shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years and liability to fine if the offence is punishable with death; imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years and liability to fine if punishable with imprisonment for life or imprisonment which may extend to ten years; or imprisonment of the description provided for the offence for a term which may extend to one-fourth part of the longest term of imprisonment provided for the offence, or with fine, or with both, if the offence is punishable with imprisonment which may extend to one year, and not to ten years. The Explanation includes acts committed outside India punishable under sections 103, 105, 307(2), (3), (4), 309(2), (3), (4), (5), 310(2), (3), (4), (5), 311, 312, 326(f) and (g), 331(4), (6), (7), (8), and 332(a) and (b), while the Exception stipulates the section shall not extend to any case in which the harbour or concealment is by the spouse of the offender.
BNS_S263:8a5a27966a94bade60f5fbd1502185c: Section 263 penalizes whoever intentionally offers any resistance or illegal obstruction to the lawful apprehension of another person for an offence, or rescues or attempts to rescue any person from lawful custody for an offence. Punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; or, if the person is charged with or liable to be apprehended for an offence punishable with imprisonment for life or imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, and shall also be liable to fine. If the person is charged/liable for an offence punishable with death, or is liable under the sentence of a Court or by virtue of a commutation to imprisonment for life or a term of ten years or upwards, the punishment is imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
